EDCS-00381059.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Flavius Titi filius Crispus veteranus legionis III Augustae ex tubicine princeps Gennarum Thamugadensium mortis suae futurae sollicitus dum advixit sibi et coniugi libertae Ulpiae Euphoretae et Flaviis Crispino et Aemerito sic filiis obsequentissimis ex HS II milibus nummum fecit hic est scul(ptus)
EDCS-00381059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Crispus, son of Titus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, formerly a trumpeter and chief of the Gennari of Thamugadi, anxious about his future death while he lived, made this for himself and his freedwoman wife Ulpia Euphoreta, and for his most dutiful sons Flavius Crispinus and Aemeritus, from two thousand sesterces. Here he is interred.
EDCS-00381059.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Titus Flavius Crispus for himself, his wife, and his sons, likely during his lifetime, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03301000.text_edition: usa Saturninus vivit sic annis LXXV signai monimenta filii sui heredes fecerunt
EDCS-03301000.translation: Saturninus lived for 75 years. His heirs made this monument for their son.
EDCS-03301000.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by the heirs of Saturninus for their son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03301001.text_edition: Dis sacrum Manibus Mettun vixit annorum sic XLV Aviania Casta fecit Magnius Secundulus
EDCS-03301001.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Mettun lived for 45 years. Aviania Casta and Magnius Secundulus made this.
EDCS-03301001.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mettun, erected by Aviania Casta and Magnius Secundulus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700824.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Invicto Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae tribunicia potestate IIII consuli II [proconsuli &
EDCS-03700824.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, father of his country, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, proconsul...
EDCS-03700824.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Emperor Gordian III, detailing his titles, likely dating to 241 AD based on his tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-04000201.translation: Marcus Claudius Marchianus lived for 51 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-04000201.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Marcus Claudius Marchianus, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04001494.text_edition: Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici consulis VI imperatoris XIIII Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus legatus Augusti pro praetore inter Augustum et Ammaederenses et Musulamios
EDCS-04001494.translation: By the authority of Imperator Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, consul for the sixth time, imperator for the fourteenth time, Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, between Augustus and the Ammaederenses and Musulamii...
EDCS-04001494.summary: This is a public inscription likely marking a boundary or official act, erected by the legate Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus under the Emperor Trajan, dating to 112 AD based on Trajan's titles.

EDCS-04901090.text_edition: Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto et Licinio Liciniano nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-04901090.translation: To our Lord Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, and to Licinius Licinianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-04901090.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring the Emperor Constantine I and Licinius II as Caesar, likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-04901091.text_edition: ddominis nnnostris Flavio Iulio Crispo et Liciniano Licinio iuniori et Flavio Claudio Constantino iuniori nobbilissimis Caessaribus II
EDCS-04901091.translation: To our Lords Flavius Julius Crispus and Licinianus Licinius Junior, and Flavius Claudius Constantinus Junior, most noble Caesars for the second time.
EDCS-04901091.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring the three Caesars Crispus, Licinius II, and Constantine II, likely dating to 317-324 AD when these three held the title of Caesar.

EDCS-04901092.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari  Aurelio  Domino nostro Constantino iuniore Caesare
EDCS-04901092.translation: To Imperator Caesar Aurelius, our Lord Constantinus Junior Caesar.
EDCS-04901092.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Constantine II, here referred to as 'Constantinus Junior Caesar,' likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05000824.text_edition: Piissimo ac clementissimo nobilissimo Caesari Marco Aurelio Numeriano Pio Felici Augusto ordo splendidissimus coloniae Lambaesitanae devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-05000824.translation: To the most pious and clement, most noble Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerianus Pius Felix Augustus, the most splendid order of the colony of Lambaesis, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-05000824.summary: This is a public dedicatory inscription by the city council of Lambaesis honoring Emperor Numerian, likely dating to 283-284 AD.

EDCS-06100629.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum totiusque domus divinae et Marci Aureli Comini Cassiani viri clarissimi legati Auggustorum pro praetore Caius Iulius Martialis et Titus Alfius Messor maggistri cum senioribus loci fecerunt dedicaverunt
EDCS-06100629.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our lords and all the divine household, and of Marcus Aurelius Cominius Cassianus, most distinguished man, imperial legate pro praetore. Gaius Iulius Martialis and Titus Alfius Messor, masters, with the elders of the place, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-06100629.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two masters and local elders, for the well-being of the emperors and a provincial governor, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-07400759.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae Marcus Acilius Mani filius Papiria Concessus aedilis praefectus iure dicundo ob honorem aedilitatis super legitimam quam rei publicae intulit adiectis at sic HS II nummum quanti pollicitus est HS II nummum ex HS IIII nummum posuit idemque dedicavit tempore aedilitatis suae decreto decurionum
EDCS-07400759.translation: To Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country. Marcus Acilius, son of Manius, of the Papirian tribe, Concessus, aedile, prefect for administering justice, on account of the honor of the aedileship, in addition to the legitimate sum he paid to the state, adding as much as he promised, 2,000 sesterces, from 4,000 sesterces, set this up and dedicated it during his aedileship by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-07400759.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Marcus Acilius Concessus to Emperor Antoninus Pius, celebrating his aedileship and his generous public contributions, erected by decree of the town council during Antoninus Pius's reign (138-161 CE).

EDCS-08100211.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Amandus, a slave born in the imperial household, lived 21 years, 3 months. He lies here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-08100211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amandus, a twenty-one-year-old imperial household slave, indicating his burial place and wishing him peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-08100222.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, a clothier, lived 87 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Felix, a clothier who lived to the age of eighty-seven.

EDCS-08100229.translation: Aemilius Felix, dearest son, lived 3 years.
EDCS-08100229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Felix, a child who died at the age of three.

EDCS-08100230.translation: Marcus Aemilius Fundanus, son of Quintus [
EDCS-08100230.summary: This is a fragmented inscription for Marcus Aemilius Fundanus, son of Quintus.

EDCS-08100231.translation: Quintus Aemilius Quietus lived 35 years. Aemilia Maxima lived 75 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100231.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Aemilius Quietus, who lived 35 years, and Aemilia Maxima, who lived 75 years, indicating Aemilia's burial place.

EDCS-08100233.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Quintilla, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-08100235.translation: Gaius Aleius Certus lived 61 years. He made this for his father Libertius.
EDCS-08100235.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Aleius Certus, who lived 61 years, created this monument for his father, Libertius.

EDCS-08100236.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Alia Fida, she lived [---] years [---].
EDCS-08100236.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Alia Fida, whose age at death is partly lost.

EDCS-08100238.translation: Ampelis lived 37 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100238.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ampelis, who lived 37 years, wishing her bones to rest well.

EDCS-08100239.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Marcus An[---], son of Marcus, Felix, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-08100239.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Marcus An[---] Felix, son of Marcus, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-08100241.translation: To the memory of Annius Milimo, who lived 60 years. Magnia Rogatula, his wife, placed this.
EDCS-08100241.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Magnia Rogatula, his wife, in memory of Annius Milimo, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08100242.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Annius Severus lived 95 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100242.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Gaius Annius Severus, who lived 95 years and is buried here.

EDCS-08100243.translation: Annia Clara lived 25 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100243.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Annia Clara, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-08100244.translation: Annia Natalis PA IFDI(?) lived 53 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100244.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annia Natalis, who lived 53 years and is buried here, with some unclear additional text.

EDCS-08100245.translation: Annia Primosa lived 35 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100245.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annia Primosa, who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-08100246.summary: This inscription records that Annia Rogata lived 35 years, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-08100247.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Antistius Rufus lived 17 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100247.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Gaius Antistius Rufus, who died at 17 years old and is buried here.

EDCS-08100248.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Antistia Procula lived 35 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100248.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Antistia Procula, who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-08100249.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antistia Stercorosa lived 40 years.
EDCS-08100249.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistia Stercorosa, likely from a tombstone, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08100250.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antistia Urbana lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100250.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistia Urbana, indicating her age and burial place.

EDCS-08100251.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Antonia Censita, who lived 80 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100251.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Censita, wishing her a peaceful rest after living 80 years.

EDCS-08100252.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Tuta, noting her remarkably long life of 100 years.

EDCS-08100253.translation: Quintus Apronius Felix lived 55 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Apronius Felix, detailing his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-08100254.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Apronius Vitalis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself while alive [and for his] wife [---].
EDCS-08100254.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Manes, prepared by Apronius Vitalis for himself and his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-08100255.translation: To the Divine Manes. Apronia Vitalis lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Apronia Vitalis, recording her age at death and burial place.

EDCS-08100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aquilia, potentially named Lucia, noting her extraordinary lifespan of 109 years.

EDCS-08100257.translation: To the memory of AR[---] Maxim[---] the daughter DEI[---] RENA[---], who lived [---] years.
EDCS-08100257.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of a woman, likely a daughter, whose full name and age are largely lost.

EDCS-08100258.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Arellius [---]RCIN[---].
EDCS-08100258.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Lucius Arellius, with much of the text missing.

EDCS-08100259.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Arruntius Manilianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-08100259.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Marcus Arruntius Manilianus, erected by or for himself.

EDCS-08100261.translation: Asinia Felicia, a most rare woman. She lived 40 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-08100261.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Asinia Felicia, highlighting her exceptional character.

EDCS-08100262.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Atilius...
EDCS-08100262.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Atilius, with further details missing.

EDCS-08100263.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Atilia Saturnina...
EDCS-08100263.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Atilia Saturnina, with significant portions of the text missing.

EDCS-08100264.translation: Aufidia Dativa set this up for her husband Aelius Adventus, her son.
EDCS-08100264.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aufidia Dativa for her husband, Aelius Adventus, who is also identified as her son, suggesting a possible family relation or adopted status.

EDCS-08100265.translation: Avilia Musibia, daughter of Lucius, lived 13 years. She is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100265.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Avilia Musibia, a young woman who died at 13, concluding with a common farewell wish.

EDCS-08100266.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aulus Nerius lived 81 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-08100266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Nerius, indicating his age and place of burial.

EDCS-08100267.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aurelius Maximus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-08100268.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Victor lived 62 years. Aurelia Cunic(a?) made this.
EDCS-08100268.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Victor, made by Aurelia Cun(ica?), presumably a relative.

EDCS-08100269.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Victor, son of Marcus, who lived years...
EDCS-08100269.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Aurelius Victor, son of Marcus, with his age at death partially preserved.

EDCS-08100270.translation: To the Divine Manes. Baebia Iucunditas lived 25 years.
EDCS-08100270.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Baebia Iucunditas, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-08100271.translation: Blasie, she is buried here, her bones rest fortunately. She lived 9 years.
EDCS-08100271.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blasie, a young girl who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-08100272.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for a child named Blatta, who lived for 5 years.

EDCS-08100273.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Bombius Ciceronianus lived 16 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100273.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Bombius Ciceronianus, a young male who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-08100274.translation: To the memory of Brinnia Ianuaria, a most rare wife. She lived 50 years.
EDCS-08100274.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Brinnia Ianuaria, who lived to be 50 years old and was remembered as an exceptional wife.

EDCS-08100275.translation: Lucius C. Emptor lived 21 years. He is placed here. C. Ianuaria, his mother, set this up.
EDCS-08100275.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Emptor, who died at 21, set up by his mother, C. Ianuaria.

EDCS-08100276.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caecilius Maro lived 15 years.
EDCS-08100276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Maro, a young male who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-08100277.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Caecilius Optatus. He lived 31 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100277.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Optatus, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-08100278.translation: Sextus Caecilius Potitus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, is buried here. He lived 30 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Caecilius Potitus, a man of the Quirina tribe who lived 30 years, ending with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100279.translation: Gaius Caecilius Urbanus lived 38 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Caecilius Urbanus, who died at the age of 38.

EDCS-08100280.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Caecilius [son?] of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, [---]vianus, lived for [1]0 years.
EDCS-08100280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young male, Lucius Caecilius [---]vianus, who lived for ten years, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-08100281.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caecilia Casia lived for five years.
EDCS-08100281.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Caecilia Casia, who lived for only five years.

EDCS-08100282.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caecilia Cicada lived for 55 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08100282.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Cicada, who lived to the age of 55, with a common valedictory formula.

EDCS-08100283.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Caecilia Euporia, my wife, who lived for 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100283.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Euporia, a wife who lived 45 years, likely erected by her husband.

EDCS-08100284.translation: To the Divine Manes. Celestina lived for 5 years.
EDCS-08100284.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celestina, a child who lived for five years.

EDCS-08100285.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caelia Cilopatria lived for 71 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100285.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Cilopatria, who lived to the age of 71.

EDCS-08100286.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesennius Minnulus lived for 50 years. His bones are here.
EDCS-08100286.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesennius Minnulus, who lived 50 years, marking the burial place of his remains.

EDCS-08100287.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesia Novicia lived for 60 years.
EDCS-08100287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia Novicia, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-08100288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Camurius Ira[---]
EDCS-08100288.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual named Camurius Ira[---].

EDCS-08100289.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Caninius Ianuarius lived for 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100289.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caninius Ianuarius, who lived to the age of 25, marking his burial place.

EDCS-08100291.translation: Carisia Marcella, daughter of Titus, lived for 90 years.
EDCS-08100291.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of death for Carisia Marcella, daughter of Titus, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-08100292.translation: To the Divine Manes. Carpus lived for 73 years. He is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100292.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Carpus, stating his age at death, his burial place, and including a common wish for his peaceful rest, addressed to the underworld gods.

EDCS-08100293.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Cassius Restutus, noting his name and age at death.

EDCS-08100294.translation: To the memory of Cassia Castula, mother, who lived for 101 years. Her sons placed this.
EDCS-08100294.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by sons to their mother, Cassia Castula, commemorating her exceptionally long life of 101 years.

EDCS-08100295.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cassia Dubitata lived for 35 years.
EDCS-08100295.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for Cassia Dubitata, stating her name and age at death.

EDCS-08100296.translation: Cassia Rufina, daughter of Lucius, lived for 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100296.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cassia Rufina, daughter of Lucius, recording her age at death and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100298.translation: To the Divine Manes. Catullina lived for [---] years.
EDCS-08100298.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for Catullina, with her age at death missing due to damage.

EDCS-08100299.translation: To the Divine Manes. Calidia(?) Castula lived for 3 years.
EDCS-08100299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for a young child, Calidia(?) Castula, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-08100300.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus(?) Clodius(?), son of Quintus(?)...
EDCS-08100300.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods, likely for a Quintus Clodius, with much of the text missing.

EDCS-08100301.translation: Clodia Silvana, daughter of Lucius, lived for 60 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-08100301.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Silvana, daughter of Lucius, recording her age at death and indicating her burial place.

EDCS-08100303.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Cominus Ces[...]
EDCS-08100303.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Lucius Cominus, with the end of his name or other details missing, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100304.translation: To the Divine Manes. Conductor lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100304.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for a man named Conductor, who lived for 40 years and is buried at this spot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100305.translation: ] Cornelius Astarius lived 25 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-08100305.summary: This is a funerary inscription, part of which is missing, for Cornelius Astarius, who lived 25 years, expressing a wish for him to rest well, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100306.translation: Cornelius Florentinus lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Florentinus, who lived to be 75 years old and is buried at this location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100308.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Cereola lived 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100308.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Cornelia Cereola, who lived 50 years and is buried at this location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100309.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Germanila lived 2 years. Cornelius Primus and Atilia Ger[manae] their daughter.
EDCS-08100309.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Cornelia Germanila, who died at the age of two, erected by her parents Cornelius Primus and Atilia German(a?), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100310.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Libosa, a wife content with her husband, lived 75 years. May your bones rest well.

EDCS-08100311.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cosinius Novellus lived 15 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100311.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Cosinius Novellus, who lived 15 years and is buried at this spot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100312.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cosinia, daughter of Italus, lived 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100312.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Cosinia, the daughter of Italus, who lived 40 years and is buried at this location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100313.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Crepereius Iucundus lived 51 years.
EDCS-08100313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Crepereius Iucundus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-08100314.translation: Crescensgentia(?). To Cristenis, his dearest daughter, who lived 2 years. Ipsanius Novellus placed this.
EDCS-08100314.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ipsanius Novellus for his dearest daughter Cristenis, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-08100316.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Domitius Pocosus lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitius Pocosus, noting his age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-08100317.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Domitius Sisario lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100317.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Domitius Sisario, stating his age at death and interment location.

EDCS-08100318.translation: Domitia Mansueta lived 25 years, 6 months. May it be well with you.
EDCS-08100318.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Mansueta, recording her age at death and a valedictory wish.

EDCS-08100319.translation: Domitia Matrona, daughter of Gnaeus, lived 23 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100319.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Matrona, daughter of Gnaeus, noting her age at death, place of burial, and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100320.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Domitia Quirilla lived 99 years.

EDCS-08100471.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintia Cyrilla lived 31 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100471.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintia Cyrilla, set up by an unspecified dedicant, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100473.translation: To the memory of Romanus, who lived 2 years, 5 months, 22 days. Ianuaria, his mother.
EDCS-08100473.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the memory of Romanus, a young child, by his mother Ianuaria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100474.translation: Roscia Maximina, daughter of Sextus, lived 10 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100474.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Roscia Maximina, the daughter of Sextus, who died at a young age, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100475.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rusticinus, who died at the age of 13, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100477.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sallustia Rogata lived 20 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100477.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sallustia Rogata, set up by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100478.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sallustia Saturnina lived 25 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100478.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sallustia Saturnina, set up by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100479.translation: Salsa lived 30 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100479.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Salsa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100480.translation: To the Divine Manes. Salvidenia Luciosa lived 35 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-08100480.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Salvidenia Luciosa, set up by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100481.translation: Salvide[nia] Marcell[ina] lived 111 years. May the earth be light upon your bones.
EDCS-08100481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Salvidenia Marcellina, who lived to an exceptional age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100483.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Scribonius Cosmus lived 80 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-08100483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Scribonius Cosmus, set up by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100485.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sempronius Candidus lived 101 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100485.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sempronius Candidus, placed by an unknown dedicant, recording his age and burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100486.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sempronia Peculiaris, also known as Anpamilla, lived 41 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08100486.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronia Peculiaris (Anpamilla), noting her age and burial, with the dedicant unmentioned, probably from the Imperial era.

EDCS-08100487.translation: Quintus Sittius Exactor, son, lived 25 years.
EDCS-08100487.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sittius Exactor, possibly placed by his father (given 'f(ilius?)'), recording his age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100488.translation: To the memory of Quintus Sittius Gemellus, a brave and good boy. Quintus Sittius Gemellus, his father, and Iulia Urbana placed this for their dearest son. He lived 13 years, 10 months. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100488.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Sittius Gemellus (the father) and Iulia Urbana to their beloved son, Quintus Sittius Gemellus, recording his premature death and a wish for peaceful rest, characteristic of Imperial-era epitaphs.

EDCS-08100489.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Sittius Ianuarius lived 80 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100489.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Ianuarius, stating his age and burial location, likely commissioned by a family member or associate during the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100490.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sittius Martialis lived 100 years; he lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100490.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittius Martialis, recording his advanced age and burial, with a common valedictory wish, typical of Imperial Roman funerary practice.

EDCS-08100491.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sittius Primus lies here; he lived 70 years.
EDCS-08100491.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittius Primus, indicating his burial and age, presumably set up by his family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100492.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sittius Rusticus lived 30 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100492.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Rusticus, marking his burial place and age at death, likely erected by his family in the Imperial era.

EDCS-08100493.translation: Quintus Sittius Victor lived 14 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100493.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sittius Victor, noting his young age and burial, with the dedicant not explicitly named, characteristic of Imperial-era epitaphs.

EDCS-08100494.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sittius Vitalis lived [X] years.
EDCS-08100494.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Sittius Vitalis, acknowledging the Divine Spirits and his age, with the exact age and burial information partially lost, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100495.translation: Sittia Exorata, she lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Exorata, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-08100497.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Felix lived 5 years. May she rest well here.
EDCS-08100497.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Felix, a child who died at age five, expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100498.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Gemelina lived 18 years.
EDCS-08100498.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Gemelina, who died at the age of eighteen.

EDCS-08100499.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Monula lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100499.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Monula, stating her age at death and her burial place.

EDCS-08100500.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Mulsula lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100500.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Mulsula, recording her age at death and her burial location.

EDCS-08100501.translation: Sittia Mustia lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100501.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Mustia, indicating her age at death and that she is buried there.

EDCS-08100503.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucia Sittia Rustica lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100503.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucia Sittia Rustica, noting her age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-08100504.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Sosius Fructus, a most holy and most pious man, who lived [missing number] years. Successus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-08100504.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Successus, a brother, to Sosius Fructus, described as a most holy and pious man, with his age partially missing.

EDCS-08100506.translation: Publius Tarquinius Philodespotus lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100506.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Tarquinius Philodespotus, indicating his age at death and that he is buried at this location.

EDCS-08100507.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Tatius Felix lived 45 years. Sittia Maxima placed this for her husband.
EDCS-08100507.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Sittia Maxima to her husband, Lucius Tatius Felix, recording his age at death.

EDCS-08100509.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titinius Episucus lived 62 years.
EDCS-08100509.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Titinius Episucus.

EDCS-08100510.translation: Marcus Titinius Pontesis lived 91 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100510.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Titinius Pontesis, noting his age, place of burial, and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100511.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tituria Vnria.
EDCS-08100511.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Tituria Vnria.

EDCS-08100512.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Trebia Contenta lived 95 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100512.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Trebia Contenta, stating her age and place of burial.

EDCS-08100513.translation: In memory of Tutia Eutychia, who is also Lucilla, who lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100513.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Tutia Eutychia, also known as Lucilla, mentioning her age and place of burial.

EDCS-08100514.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valeria Sittoria lived 85 years.
EDCS-08100514.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Sittoria, stating her age at death.

EDCS-08100515.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Vasidia Saturnina lived 35 years.
EDCS-08100515.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vasidia Saturnina, recording her name and age.

EDCS-08100516.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Veneria lived 12 years (or 62, 72, 82).
EDCS-08100516.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Veneria, noting her age at death, though the exact number of years is partially illegible.

EDCS-08100517.translation: Quintus Vettius Saturninus lived 24 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100517.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Vettius Saturninus, mentioning his age and place of burial.

EDCS-08100520.translation: Vibieia, daughter of Gaius, Seveila, lived 9 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100520.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vibieia Seveila, daughter of Gaius, indicating her young age at death, her place of burial, and a valedictory wish.

EDCS-08100522.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vitalis lived 50 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-08100522.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitalis, noting his age and burial place.

EDCS-08100524.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Volumnius Ianuarius lived 55 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-08100524.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Volumnius Ianuarius, detailing his name, age, and burial.

EDCS-08100525.translation: Publius Volumnius Novellus lived 95 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-08100525.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Volumnius Novellus, recording his long life and burial.

EDCS-08100526.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Colussius Felix, stating his name and age at death.

EDCS-08100527.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Volusius Fortunatus, pious, lived 55 years.
EDCS-08100527.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Volusius Fortunatus, noting his piety and age.

EDCS-08300572.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepote Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate V consule III patre patriae Publius Metilius Secundus legatus Augusti pro praetore patronus coloniae dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-08300572.translation: When Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, was emperor; Publius Metilius Secundus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power and patron of the colony, dedicated this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-08300572.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a public work or building by Publius Metilius Secundus, legate and patron of a colony, during the reign of Emperor Hadrian, funded by public money and decreed by the decurions.

EDCS-08300575.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepote divi Nervae pronepote Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patre patriae imperatore II pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XIIII consule IIII plateam stratam Marcus Valerius Etruscus legatus Augusti pro praetore patronus coloniae dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-08300575.translation: When Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of divine Nerva, father of the country, imperator for the second time, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, was emperor; Marcus Valerius Etruscus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power and patron of the colony, dedicated this paved street by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-08300575.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a paved street by Marcus Valerius Etruscus, legate and patron of a colony, during the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius, funded by public money and decreed by the decurions.

EDCS-08600659.text_edition: Chioni memoriae Publi Exoppi Publi filii Quirina Nivalis quaestoris aedilis IIIviri quinquennalis omnibusque honoribus IIII coloniarum functi vixit annos LXXV Chioni Exoppia Saturnina patri incomparabili
EDCS-08600659.translation: To the memory of Chioni and Publius Exoppus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, Nivalis, quaestor, aedile, triumvir, quinquennalis, and having held all the honors of four colonies, who lived 75 years. Chioni Exoppia Saturnina [set this up] for her incomparable father.
EDCS-08600659.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Chioni Exoppia Saturnina to her father, Publius Exoppus, a prominent magistrate who held numerous offices in four colonies, and to Chioni.

EDCS-08600660.text_edition: Marco Pontio Marci filio Pupinia Varano Sabino tribuno laticlavio militum legionis III Augustae XVviro sacris faciundis Xviro stlitibus iudicandis VIviro turmae equitum Romanorum signiferi legionis eiusdem
EDCS-08600660.translation: For Marcus Pontius, son of Marcus, of the Pupinia tribe, Varanus Sabinus, military tribune of laticlave rank for the Third Augustan Legion, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, decemvir for judging lawsuits, sexvir of a troop of Roman equites, standard-bearer of the same legion.
EDCS-08600660.summary: This inscription commemorates the illustrious career of Marcus Pontius Varanus Sabinus, detailing his military and priestly offices as well as judicial and equestrian roles.

EDCS-08600661.text_edition: Dianae Augustae pro salute Imperatorum dominorum nostrorum Invictissimorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Augustorum et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti et senatus et castrorum curia Commodiana  curiales curiae Commodianae Lucius Maenius Vibidianus Marcus Annius Faustus Caius Popilius Laetus Lucius Longeiius sic Primus Lucius Licinius Honoratus Marcus Volussenius Fortunatus Quintus Caecilius Ateanus Quintus Timinius Felix Quintus Lurius Severus Publius Caelius Saturninus Tiberius Claudius Victor Lucius Iulius Donatus magister Lucius Statius Maximus Marcus Cornelius Servandianus Caius Clodius Saturninus Lucius Aulonius Felix Lucius Valerius Victor Caius Albanius Aelianus Publius Aelius Victor Marcus Aennius Saturninus Leporinus Marcus Acilius Lucanus Quintus Sallustius Candidus Quintus Iulius Thelemachus  Quintus Helvius Muricus Quintus Iulius Mychronius Caius Publilius Fortunatus Marcus Ulpius Felix Lucius Aedius Ianuarius Lucius Caeladius Saturninus Quintus Grattius Fortunatus Marcus Antonius Saturninus Lucius Egnatius Agricola Marcus Ulpius Peculiaris Publius Pactimeius Felix Quintus Antonius Sulla Marcus Acilius Celadianus Publius Fabius Catulianus Quintus Iulius Sapa Lucius Aemilius Victor Caius Annius Posianus Lucius Claudius Festivus Titus Flavius Cuminus Lucius Aemilius Donatus Quintus Pomponius Geminus Quintus Grattius Donatus Lucius Blossius Honoratus Quintus Cornelius Victor Lucius Valerius Felix Quintus Iulius Fortunatus Publius Camerius Faustus Caius Peticius Castus Quintus Arrius Processus Stablicus
EDCS-08600661.translation: To Diana Augusta, for the safety of our most unconquered Lords, Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and of Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor and of the senate and of the camps. The Commodiana curia, and the curials of the Commodiana curia: Lucius Maenius Vibidianus, Marcus Annius Faustus, Gaius Popilius Laetus, Lucius Longeius Primus, Lucius Licinius Honoratus, Marcus Volussenius Fortunatus, Quintus Caecilius Ateanus, Quintus Timinius Felix, Quintus Lurius Severus, Publius Caelius Saturninus, Tiberius Claudius Victor, Lucius Iulius Donatus (master), Lucius Statius Maximus, Marcus Cornelius Servandianus, Gaius Clodius Saturninus, Lucius Aulonius Felix, Lucius Valerius Victor, Gaius Albanius Aelianus, Publius Aelius Victor, Marcus Aennius Saturninus Leporinus, Marcus Acilius Lucanus, Quintus Sallustius Candidus, Quintus Iulius Thelemachus, Quintus Helvius Muricus, Quintus Iulius Mychronius, Gaius Publilius Fortunatus, Marcus Ulpius Felix, Lucius Aedius Ianuarius, Lucius Caeladius Saturninus, Quintus Grattius Fortunatus, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, Lucius Egnatius Agricola, Marcus Ulpius Peculiaris, Publius Pactimeius Felix, Quintus Antonius Sulla, Marcus Acilius Celadianus, Publius Fabius Catulianus, Quintus Iulius Sapa, Lucius Aemilius Victor, Gaius Annius Posianus, Lucius Claudius Festivus, Titus Flavius Cuminus, Lucius Aemilius Donatus, Quintus Pomponius Geminus, Quintus Grattius Donatus, Lucius Blossius Honoratus, Quintus Cornelius Victor, Lucius Valerius Felix, Quintus Iulius Fortunatus, Publius Camerius Faustus, Gaius Peticius Castus, Quintus Arrius Processus Stablicus.
EDCS-08600661.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Diana Augusta for the well-being of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, Empress Julia Augusta, the senate, and the military camps, made by the Commodiana curia and its numerous members.

EDCS-08901193.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo conservatori domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Quinti Anici Fausti legati Augusti pro praetore   AED IPAN Severo et Herenniano consulibus Acilius Felix Imperatore Commodo II et Vero consulibus Iulius Saim Imperatore Commodo V consule Aemilius Saturninus Falco et Claro consulibus Iulius Donatus Geminius Emeritus Tertullo et Clemente consulibus Arrius
EDCS-08901193.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, preserver of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus and Quintus Anicius Faustus, imperial legate propraetor. Severus and Herennianus being consuls; Acilius Felix, Emperor Commodus for the second time and Verus being consuls; Julius Saim, Emperor Commodus for the fifth time being consul; Aemilius Saturninus, Falco and Clarus being consuls; Julius Donatus, Geminius Emeritus, Tertullus and Clemens being consuls; Arrius...
EDCS-08901193.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely erected by various individuals or groups during several different consulships, spanning from the reign of Commodus to Septimius Severus.

EDCS-09300564.text_edition: Genio TFLZI Augusto pro salute Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti fili eius Quintus Homullius Victor et Caius Apuleius Gratianus magistri sua pecunia donum dederunt Laterano et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-09300564.translation: To the August Genius, for the welfare of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, his son. Quintus Homullius Victor and Gaius Apuleius Gratianus, masters, gave this gift at their own expense, Lateranus and Rufinus being consuls.
EDCS-09300564.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by two masters to an August Genius for the health of Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla in 213 AD.

EDCS-09700396.text_edition: Sabino et Venusto Luceius Felix vixit annos LXVIIII heredes patri fecerunt
EDCS-09700396.translation: Sabinus and Venustus being consuls. Luceius Felix lived 69 years. His heirs made this for their father.
EDCS-09700396.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Luceius Felix, erected by his heirs in 290 AD.

EDCS-09700785.text_edition: Deo magno sacrum Caius Gargilius Zabo intravit sacerdos Saturni annis LXV votum libens animo solvit pecora VIIII agnum taurum verbecem oviculam gallinas V
EDCS-09700785.translation: Sacred to the Great God. Gaius Gargilius Zabo entered as a priest of Saturn for 65 years. He willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow, offering nine heads of cattle: a lamb, a bull, a wether, a ewe, and five chickens.
EDCS-09700785.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Gargilius Zabo, a priest of Saturn for 65 years, to the Great God, listing his animal offerings.

EDCS-09800387.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco Medico Parthico Germanico patri patriae imperatori VII tribunicia potestate XXVI consuli III dedicante Marco Aemilio Macro Saturnino legato Augusti pro praetore plateam Lambaesitani straverunt decreto decurionum
EDCS-09800387.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Armeniacus, Medicus, Parthicus, Germanicus, father of the country, imperator for the seventh time, holding tribunician power for the twenty-sixth time, consul for the third time, with Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus, imperial legate propraetor, dedicating it. The citizens of Lambaesis paved the square by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800387.summary: This inscription records the paving of a square in Lambaesis by its citizens, dedicated to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus (Caracalla) under the supervision of the imperial legate, and dated to 213 AD.

EDCS-09800389.text_edition: Pro salute et incolumitate domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Augusti Pii Felicis et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae cultores dei Ierhobolis iuniores constituti Praesente et Extricato II consulibus Idibus Martis fecerunt magistri cultorum qui nomina sua subiecerunt dedicaverunt ius Victor [ &
EDCS-09800389.translation: For the welfare and safety of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, and Julia Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps, and of the senate and of the homeland. The junior devotees of the god Ierhobolis, established in office, made this on the Ides of March, with Praesens and Extricatus for the second time being consuls. The masters of the devotees, who submitted their names, dedicated it: Victor, Ius...
EDCS-09800389.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the junior devotees of the god Ierhobolis for the welfare of Emperor Caracalla, Julia Augusta, the army, the senate, and the homeland, dated to 217 AD.

EDCS-09800391.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus, legate.
EDCS-09800391.summary: This is a short dedicatory inscription to Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus, likely an imperial legate.

EDCS-09800392.text_edition: Lucio Vespronio Candido Sallustio Sabiniano consuli speculatores et beneficiarii eius
EDCS-09800392.translation: To Lucius Vespronius Candidus Sallustius Sabinianus, consul. His scouts and beneficiaries.
EDCS-09800392.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the scouts and beneficiaries to Lucius Vespronius Candidus Sallustius Sabinianus, likely in his capacity as consul.

EDCS-09800618.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Iulius Marcellus, a most simple man. He lived 90 years. He lies here. Iulia Matutina made this for her father.
EDCS-09800618.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Iulius Marcellus, erected by his daughter Iulia Matutina.

EDCS-09800676.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Magnius Oitus, son of Lucius. He lived 28 years.
EDCS-09800676.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Magnius Oitus, son of Lucius, who lived for 28 years.

EDCS-09800692.translation: To the Divine Manes. Of Aufidius Euphrosynus. He lived 60 years. He is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800692.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aufidius Euphrosynus, indicating his age and a wish for his peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800693.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cornelia Lucida. Urbanus, her husband, lived 60 years.
EDCS-09800693.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Urbanus to his wife, Cornelia Lucida, stating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800694.translation: Faius Gallus Oxten(?). He lived 63 years.
EDCS-09800694.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Faius Gallus Oxten, noting his age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800695.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Galerius Marcellus lived for SFMA(?) years.
EDCS-09800695.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Galerius Marcellus, mentioning his years of life with an unclear numerical value, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800698.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Iulia Iuliosa, stating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800699.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Mara(?) Aecilia(?). She lived 121 years.
EDCS-09800699.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mara Aecilia, notable for her exceptionally long stated lifespan, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800701.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Claudius Musteolus, indicating his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800702.translation: To the Divine Manes. Nonius Crescens lived 90 years.
EDCS-09800702.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nonius Crescens, stating his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09800706.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Publius Antonius Publi filius Quirina Antoninus aedilis designatus signum marmoreum cultoribus Dianae et Mercuri sua pecunia dedit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-09800706.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Publius Antonius Antoninus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, aedile designate, dedicated a marble statue to the worshippers of Diana and Mercury at his own expense and likewise dedicated it.
EDCS-09800706.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a marble statue to Diana Augusta and the worshippers of Diana and Mercury by Publius Antonius Antoninus, an aedile designate, at his own expense.

EDCS-09800710.summary: This brief inscription identifies Gaius Iulius by his name and the public office of aedile, possibly part of a larger dedication or marker.

EDCS-09800711.text_edition: Lucius Manilius Luci filius Quirina  filiorum suorum po[su&
EDCS-09800711.translation: Lucius Manilius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, dedicated this for his sons.
EDCS-09800711.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by Lucius Manilius, of the Quirina tribe, for his deceased sons, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09800712.text_edition: Quinto Bottio Quinti filio Quirina Senecioni filio
EDCS-09800712.translation: To Quintus Bottius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, to his son Senecio.
EDCS-09800712.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Bottius to his son Senecio, both of the Quirina tribe, probably from the early Imperial era.

EDCS-09800713.translation: Senecio of the Quirina tribe, from 11,000 sesterces.

EDCS-09801894.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Sittius Marcianus vixit annos XXV
EDCS-09801894.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Sittius Marcianus lived twenty-five years.
EDCS-09801894.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Marcus Sittius Marcianus.

EDCS-09801895.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Sittius Victor vixit annos LXXI
EDCS-09801895.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Sittius Victor lived seventy-one years.
EDCS-09801895.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, commemorating Gaius Sittius Victor and his age.

EDCS-12500224.translation: To Mother Earth, to Heraecura, to the Great Idaean Mother, Publius Sextilius Honoratus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina voting tribe, initiated and performed the taurobolium and criobolium, and set up an altar.
EDCS-12500224.summary: This inscription records an altar dedicated to multiple deities by Publius Sextilius Honoratus after he performed the taurobolium and criobolium rituals.

EDCS-12900010.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Geminia Ptolemais lived sixty-five years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900010.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminia Ptolemais, noting her age and including a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900011.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lycera lived three years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900011.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for a young girl named Lycera, stating her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900012.translation: Grania Mura lived forty years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900012.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Grania Mura, indicating her age and burial place.

EDCS-12900013.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Grania Natalis lived five years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900013.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child, Grania Natalis, recording her age, burial, and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900014.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Grania Nigella lived twenty-seven years.
EDCS-12900014.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Grania Nigella, stating her age at death.

EDCS-12900016.translation: To the memory of the Helvior. Gaius Helvius Gemellus and Antonia Mustica, his wife, made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-12900016.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for a family tomb, made by Gaius Helvius Gemellus and Antonia Mustica for themselves and their descendants during their lifetimes.

EDCS-12900017.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Heraclida lived twenty-seven years.
EDCS-12900017.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Heraclida and noting her age at death.

EDCS-12900018.translation: Lucius Herennius Fortunatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-12900018.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Herennius Fortunatus, who lived 60 years, with a common valedictory formula.

EDCS-12900019.translation: To the Divine Manes. Hilaria lived 45 years.
EDCS-12900019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan of a woman named Hilaria.

EDCS-12900020.translation: To the memory of Quintus Hirrius Martialis, dearest son. He is buried here. He lived 2 years.
EDCS-12900020.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Hirrius Martialis, a very young son who died at the age of two.

EDCS-12900021.translation: To the memory of Lligia(?) Honorata. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-12900021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lligia Honorata, who lived 60 years, though the first part of her name is uncertain due to a restoration.

EDCS-12900025.translation: To the Divine Manes. Horatia Monnula of the Quirina tribe (?).
EDCS-12900025.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Horatia Monnula, possibly indicating her tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12900026.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Hordionius Salutaris lived 8 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900026.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Hordionius Salutaris, a child who lived only eight years.

EDCS-12900027.translation: To the Divine Manes of Decimus Iulius Crescentianus. He lived 20 years. He is buried here. To the Divine Manes of Iulia Honorata, sweetest wife (?). She lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900027.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, commemorating Decimus Iulius Crescentianus, who lived 20 years, and Iulia Honorata, his wife, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-12900028.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Gemellus lived 56 years.
EDCS-12900028.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Gaius Iulius Gemellus, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-12900029.translation: Of Gaius Iulius Honoratus Iunior, his brother [---] lived [---] years.
EDCS-12900029.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Honoratus Iunior, possibly set up by his brother, with the age of the deceased missing.

EDCS-12900030.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Iulius Victoricus lived 51 years.
EDCS-12900030.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Quintus Iulius Victoricus, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-12900031.translation: To the memory of Januarius, sweetest son, who lived 10 months and 1 day.
EDCS-12900031.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Januarius, a young son, likely erected by his grieving parents in the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900034.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Iotelus Citirvarius lived 81 years. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you happily.
EDCS-12900034.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Titus Iotelus Citirvarius, an individual who lived to an advanced age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900035.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Gaius Irius Catrvarius, recording his age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900037.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Iulius Agathangelus, who lived 30 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900037.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Gaius Iulius Agathangelus, an individual who died at 30 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900038.translation: Gaius Iulius Amsiginus, 35 years old. He lies here.
EDCS-12900038.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Amsiginus, recording his age and burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900039.translation: Lucius Iulius, son of Lucius, of the [---] tribe, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-12900039.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius, the son of Lucius, noting his filiation and part of his tribal affiliation, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900040.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Bacquas lived 51 years. To the Divine Manes. Iulius Ingenuus lived 21 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900040.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Iulius Bacquas and Iulius Ingenuus, recording their respective ages at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900041.translation: Marcus Iulius Bitus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 55 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900041.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Bitus, noting his full name, filiation, tribe, and age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Iulius Bitus lived 42 years.
EDCS-12900042.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Marcus Iulius Bitus, recording his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900043.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Crescens lived 12 years.
EDCS-12900043.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Gaius Iulius Crescens, a young individual who died at age 12, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900044.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Iulius Crescenus lived 20 years.
EDCS-12900044.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Iulius Crescenus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-12900045.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Demetrius lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Demetrius, noting he lived 30 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-12900046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Dometianus, son of Quintus. MND Q I V S.
EDCS-12900046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Iulius Dometianus, son of Quintus, with additional abbreviations of uncertain meaning.

EDCS-12900047.translation: Lucius Iulius Episucus lived 11 years. To the Divine Shades. Lucius Iulius Kandidus lived 80 years, who truly lived. May your bones rest well. To the Divine Shades. To Sittia Spenis; whoever loves his wife, let him unite his love with this example. Indeed, it is a sweet solace of life. She departed to the gods above with her most beloved son Episucus. She lived 57 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900047.summary: This is a complex funerary monument dedicated to three individuals: Lucius Iulius Episucus (died at 11), Lucius Iulius Kandidus (died at 80), and Sittia Spenis (died at 57) who is noted to have departed with her son Episucus, likely indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-12900048.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Fatalis lived 90 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900048.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Fatalis, who lived to the age of 90.

EDCS-12900049.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Fatalis lived 36 years.
EDCS-12900049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Fatalis, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-12900050.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Faustianus lived [?] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Faustianus, noting he is buried here, with his age partially illegible.

EDCS-12900051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Felix, stating he lived 65 years.

EDCS-12900052.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, most skilled in all studies, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Felix, a talented individual who lived 20 years and was a member of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-12900053.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Felix lived 32 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Felix, who lived 32 years and is buried here.

EDCS-12900054.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Felix lived 40 years.
EDCS-12900054.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Iulius Felix, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-12900055.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Iulius Fortis at death, which was 65 years.

EDCS-12900056.translation: Iulius Fortunatus lived 40 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Fortunatus, noting he lived for 40 years and includes a common valediction for the deceased's bones.

EDCS-12900057.translation: To Gaius Iulius Fuscus, son of Gaius. He lived 10 years.
EDCS-12900057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Fuscus, son of Gaius, who lived to be 10 years old.

EDCS-12900058.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to a Marcus Iulius with an unclear cognomen beginning with 'Gem'.

EDCS-12900059.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Gudulus lived 65 years.
EDCS-12900059.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Iulius Gudulus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12900060.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Hospes placed this for his dearest brother. He lived 15 years. Buried here.
EDCS-12900060.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Hospes to his dearest brother, who lived 15 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-12900061.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Hospitalis lived 20 years.
EDCS-12900061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Iulius Hospitalis, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-12900062.translation: Gaius Iulius Hospitalis, son of Titus, lived 15 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900062.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Hospitalis, son of Titus, who lived 15 years and is buried here, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900063.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius [...] Surullio is buried here. He lived 10 years.
EDCS-12900063.summary: This is a sacred funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Surullio, who lived 10 years and is buried at this site.

EDCS-12900064.translation: Marcus Iulius Iulianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 26 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Iulianus, who lived 26 years.

EDCS-12900065.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Iulius Liberalis lived 91 years.
EDCS-12900065.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Marcus Iulius Liberalis, who lived to an old age.

EDCS-12900067.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Monnosus lived 35 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie light upon your bones.
EDCS-12900067.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Gaius Iulius Monnosus, wishing him eternal rest.

EDCS-12900068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Novatus, noting his age at death.

EDCS-12900069.translation: Gaius Iulius Onesimus lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Onesimus, indicating his age and burial place.

EDCS-12900070.translation: Marcus Iulius Oriclo lived 16 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900070.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Oriclo, noting his young age at death.

EDCS-12900071.translation: Iulius Pacatus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 32 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Pacatus, indicating his tribal affiliation and age.

EDCS-12900072.translation: Gaius Iulius Pius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 105 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900072.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Pius, remarkable for his very long life and noted ancestry.

EDCS-12900073.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Pudens lived 101 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900073.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Gaius Iulius Pudens, who lived over a century.

EDCS-12900074.translation: [I]ulius Quintus lived 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Quintus, noting his age and burial.

EDCS-12900075.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Restutian, he lived 21 years. He rests here.
EDCS-12900075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Restutian, who lived for 21 years.

EDCS-12900076.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Rogatus, he lived 27 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900076.summary: This inscription records the burial of Iulius Rogatus, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-12900077.translation: Gaius Iulius Rufus, he lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900077.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Gaius Iulius Rufus, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-12900078.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Rusticus, he lived 8 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900078.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Rusticus, who died at the young age of 8.

EDCS-12900079.translation: Lucius Iulius Saturninus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, he lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900079.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucius Iulius Saturninus, son of Gaius, a member of the Quirina tribe, who lived to be 35 years old.

EDCS-12900080.translation: Iulius Serenus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, he lived 17 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Serenus, son of Lucius and from the Quirina tribe, who died at 17 years of age.

EDCS-12900081.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Iulius Severian, he lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900081.summary: This inscription marks the burial of Publius Iulius Severian, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-12900083.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Istatian, he lived 75 years. He is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Aemilian, he lived 80 years.
EDCS-12900083.summary: This inscription records the burials of two individuals, Quintus Iulius Istatian (75 years old) and Quintus Iulius Aemilian (80 years old), likely on a shared monument.

EDCS-12900084.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Tauriscus, he lived 70 years.
EDCS-12900084.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Iulius, son of Tauriscus, who lived for 70 years, likely a funerary record.

EDCS-12900085.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Titus, he lived 42 years.
EDCS-12900085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Titus, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-12900086.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Victor lived 14 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Iulius Victor, recording his age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900087.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Victor. C. Camurnius, his sons and grandsons, while safe and sound, placed this memorial to their sweetest father. He lived 71 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900087.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the sons and grandsons of Iulius Victor to their beloved father, recording his age and the fact of his burial.

EDCS-12900088.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Victorinus lived 100 years.
EDCS-12900088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Victorinus, noting his remarkable age of 100 years.

EDCS-12900089.translation: To the memory of Gaius Iulius Victorinus Solacius. He lived 6 years.
EDCS-12900089.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Gaius Iulius Victorinus Solacius, who died at the age of six.

EDCS-12900091.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Urbanus lived 11 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Iulius Urbanus, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-12900092.translation: Lucius Iulius Urbanus, freedman of Publius, lived 70 years.
EDCS-12900092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Urbanus, a freedman of Publius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12900093.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-12900093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia, identified as the daughter of Gaius, with no age or further details provided.

EDCS-12900094.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Aenia, recording her age as 70 years.

EDCS-12900095.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Antonia lived 11 years.
EDCS-12900095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Antonia, who died at the young age of eleven.

EDCS-12900096.translation: Iulia Anulla(?), daughter of Publius, lived 50 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Anulla, daughter of Publius, who lived 50 years, including a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900097.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Castula lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900097.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Castula, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-12900098.translation: Iulia Cellaria lived 70 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900098.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Iulia Cellaria, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-12900099.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Cirtesia, daughter of Gaius, lived 25 years.
EDCS-12900099.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Cirtesia, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-12900100.translation: Iulia Cirtesia lived 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900100.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Cirtesia, who lived 50 years, wishing her bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900101.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the remarkably long life of Iulia Cirtesia, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-12900102.translation: Iulia Crispilla, daughter of Gaius, lived 21 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Crispilla, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 21, including a common wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900103.translation: Iulia Ecloge, freedwoman of Publius, lived 70 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900103.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Ecloge, a freedwoman of Publius, who lived to the age of 70.

EDCS-12900105.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Fatalis lived 18 years. She is here.
EDCS-12900105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Fatalis, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-12900106.translation: Iulia Faustina lived 1 year and 1 month. She lies here. R.E. (unknown meaning).
EDCS-12900106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Faustina, a child who died at the age of 1 year and 1 month.

EDCS-12900107.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Fortunata lived 82 years.
EDCS-12900107.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Fortunata, who lived to the age of 82.

EDCS-12900108.translation: To Iulia Frustula, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-12900108.summary: This is an epitaph for Iulia Frustula, a woman who lived to the age of 80, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900109.translation: Iulia, daughter of Gaius, allitta, lived 21 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900109.summary: This epitaph commemorates Iulia, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 21, and includes a common valedictory wish for the deceased.

EDCS-12900110.translation: Iulia Gula lived 85 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900110.summary: This is an epitaph for Iulia Gula, a woman who lived 85 years, indicating a long life in Roman times.

EDCS-12900111.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Honorata lived 16 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900111.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Iulia Honorata, a young woman who died at 16 years old.

EDCS-12900112.translation: Iulia Hospita lived 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900112.summary: This epitaph commemorates Iulia Hospita, a woman who lived to 60 years old, including a formulaic wish for her eternal peace.

EDCS-12900113.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Ianuaria lived 75 years.
EDCS-12900113.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the dead for Iulia Ianuaria, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-12900114.translation: Iulia Ianuaria lived 81 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900114.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iulia Ianuaria, who lived to the advanced age of 81 years, and notes her burial place.

EDCS-12900115.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Irene lived 50 years.
EDCS-12900115.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Irene, who lived 50 years, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900116.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Lallis lived 30 years.
EDCS-12900116.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to the Divine Manes for Iulia Lallis, a woman who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-12900117.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Maia made this for Gaius Seius Primitivus, her son, who lived [an unknown number of] years.
EDCS-12900117.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Maia to her son, Gaius Seius Primitivus, who passed away at an unspecified age, under the protection of the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900118.translation: Julia Marcella lived for 30 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-12900118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Marcella, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900119.translation: Julia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, lived for 45 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-12900119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Marcella, noting her parentage, age, and burial location.

EDCS-12900120.translation: Julia Marcia, daughter of Marcus, who lived for 40 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-12900120.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Marcia, indicating her father, age, and burial place.

EDCS-12900121.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Martilla lived for 85 years.
