EDCS-12900121.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, commemorating Julia Martilla and her age.

EDCS-12900122.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia, daughter of [---], Mart[---].
EDCS-12900122.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Julia, whose filiation and name are partially preserved.

EDCS-12900123.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Matrona, recording her age.

EDCS-12900124.translation: Julia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, lived for 25 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-12900124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Maxima, noting her parentage, age, and burial.

EDCS-12900125.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Maximina lived for one year.
EDCS-12900125.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Julia Maximina, who died at one year of age.

EDCS-12900126.translation: Julia Melana lived for 90 years; she is laid to rest here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-12900126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Melana, recording her age, burial place, and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-12900127.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Monnula lived for 41 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-12900127.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Julia Monnula, detailing her age and burial location.

EDCS-12900128.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Mustia, daughter of Gaius, lived 31 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Mustia, erected by an unnamed dedicant, likely a family member, commemorating her life and burial.

EDCS-12900129.translation: Julia Nabira lived 50 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900129.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Julia Nabira, who lived to the age of 50.

EDCS-12900131.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Nirosa lived 33 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900131.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Nirosa, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900132.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Novella placed this for herself while alive.
EDCS-12900132.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a monument Julia Novella had prepared for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-12900134.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Nusanula (?) lived 30 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900134.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Nusanula, indicating her age and burial site.

EDCS-12900135.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Octavia lived 70 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900135.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Octavia, noting her age and final resting place.

EDCS-12900136.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Quetina lived for [an unspecified number of] years.
EDCS-12900136.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Julia Quetina, mentioning her dedication to the Divine Manes and the beginning of her age.

EDCS-12900137.translation: To Julia Renata, the most sacred of all women, after completing her old age, she followed her husband, Julius Sarnianus. She lived 70 years.
EDCS-12900137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Renata, celebrating her piety and devotion to her husband, Julius Sarnianus, and noting her age at death.

EDCS-12900138.translation: Julia Rericta (?) is here, 25 years old.
EDCS-12900138.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial spot of Julia Rericta, who was 25 years old.

EDCS-12900139.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Restuta lived 13 years.
EDCS-12900139.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Restuta, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-12900140.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Rogata lived 100 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900140.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Rogata, indicating her age and burial location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900141.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Rogata lived 35 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900141.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Rogata, noting her age, burial, and a wish for her peaceful rest, typical of Roman tombs from the Imperial era.

EDCS-12900144.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Silvana lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Silvana, recording her age and burial place, although with a grammatical gender mismatch in the Latin formula 'hic situs est'.

EDCS-12900145.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Iulia Spenis, who lived 11 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900145.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young Iulia Spenis, detailing her age, burial, and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-12900146.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Telesphoris, who is also known as Ingenua, lived 7 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Iulia Telesphoris, also known by the cognomen Ingenua, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-12900147.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Telesphoris lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900147.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Telesphoris, stating her age and burial location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12900148.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Venusta lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900148.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Venusta, providing her age and indicating her burial place.

EDCS-12900149.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Iulia Vicari.
EDCS-12900149.summary: This appears to be a partial funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Vicari, with no further details preserved.

EDCS-12900150.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Iulia Victoria, a Jewess (?). [---]
EDCS-12900150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Victoria, possibly identifying her as Jewish, and likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12900152.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Urbica, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900152.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Urbica, daughter of Lucius, recording her age and burial location, typical of Roman funerary monuments.

EDCS-12900153.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Iunius Mamilius lived 61 years.
EDCS-12900153.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Quintus Iunius Mamilius, who lived to be 61 years old.

EDCS-12900154.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iunia Sittiola lived 18 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iunia Sittiola, who died at the age of 18 and is interred at this location.

EDCS-12900155.translation: Istileia Faustilla, daughter of Quintus, lived 5 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Istileia Faustilla, daughter of Quintus, who died at the age of 5 and is buried here.

EDCS-12900156.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iuventia Lucilla lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iuventia Lucilla, who lived 70 years and is buried in this spot.

EDCS-12900157.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iuventia Rogata, a mother of one husband, lived 75 years; she is buried here. To the Divine Manes. Lucius Um[---], son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Restitutus, father, lived 6[3]2 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900157.summary: This monument is a double funerary inscription, dedicating tombs to Iuventia Rogata, a univira who lived 75 years, and to Lucius [---] Restitutus, a father, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 632 years (likely a scribal error for a common age like 62 or 72).

EDCS-12900158.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus L. Gemellus lived 70 years.
EDCS-12900158.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus L. Gemellus, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-12900159.translation: Marcus L. Herma lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900159.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Marcus L. Herma, who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-12900163.translation: Cemeteries for the memory of the gens Lepidia, of Lepidus Nampulus and Stennia Potita, and of their heirs, who live to their own day. Istantius made this.
EDCS-12900163.summary: This inscription records the establishment of cemeteries for the gens Lepidia, specifically for Lepidus Nampulus and Stennia Potita and their heirs, created by Istantius.

EDCS-12900164.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Licinius Castus lived 101 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900164.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Licinius Castus, who lived to the remarkable age of 101 and is buried at this site.

EDCS-12900165.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Licinius Hermes lived 45 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-12900165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Licinius Hermes, who lived 45 years, accompanied by a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900166.translation: Lucius Licinius Perennius, son of Gaius, lived 101 years. He is laid here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-12900166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Perennius, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900167.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Licinius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina voting tribe, to his most exceptional father. He lived 86 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-12900167.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Licinius Rufinus by his son, commemorating his exceptionally long life, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900168.translation: Gaius Licinius Silvanus Hanno lived 19 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-12900168.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Licinius Silvanus Hanno, a young individual, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900169.translation: To the Divine Manes. Licinius Zeius lived 60 years.
EDCS-12900169.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Licinius Zeius, indicating his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900171.translation: To the Divine Manes. Licinia Ingenua lived 45 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-12900171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Ingenua, recording her age and place of burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900172.translation: To the Divine Manes. Livia Tertullina lived 25 years.
EDCS-12900172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Livia Tertullina, noting her young age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900173.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lollius Honoratus lived 80 years.
EDCS-12900173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lollius Honoratus, recording his advanced age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900174.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Lollius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina voting tribe, an alumnus, lived 19 years.
EDCS-12900174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Lollius Rusticus, identified as an 'alumnus' (foster child or pupil), who died at a young age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900175.translation: Marcus Lollius Synecdexius lived 50 years. He is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Lollius Synecdexius, including a wish for his bones to rest well, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900176.translation: Lucida, an imperial freedwoman, is laid here, to her most obedient husband.
EDCS-12900176.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucida, an Augusti verna (a slave born into the imperial household, likely later freed), erected by or dedicated to her most obedient husband, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900177.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucida, the home-born slave of our Augustus, lived 11 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucida, a young slave of the emperor, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900178.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the name and age of Lucilia Felicia.

EDCS-12900180.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Lutatius Ingenuus, who lived 2 (?) years.
EDCS-12900180.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Lutatius Ingenuus, likely a child, noting his name and short lifespan.

EDCS-12900181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Lutatius Principinus, recording his name and advanced age.

EDCS-12900182.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Lurius Faustus, stating his name and age.

EDCS-12900183.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius M. O. lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900183.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man named Gaius, with an unclear family name, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-12900184.translation: Lucius M., son of Lucius, Urbanus, lived 30 years. His bones were interred here eight days before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-12900184.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius M. Urbanus, recording his name, age, and the date his bones were interred.

EDCS-12900185.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Aecilius Marcianus, who lived 41 years.
EDCS-12900185.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aecilius Marcianus, noting his name and age at death.

EDCS-12900186.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Mecilia Castula, a most pleasant woman, who lived 80 years. Quintus Cassius Felix (made this) for his most loving, most dutiful, most joyful sister.
EDCS-12900186.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Cassius Felix to his beloved sister, Mecilia Castula, highlighting her character and age.

EDCS-12900187.translation: To the Divine Manes. Decima Maecilia Hilara lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decima Maecilia Hilara, stating her name, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-12900188.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maecilia Onesime lived for 35 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maecilia Onesime, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900189.translation: To the Divine Manes. Manius Marcianus lived for 20 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manius Marcianus, wishing for his peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900190.translation: Marcus Magnius Felix, who is also Secretius, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-12900190.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Magnius Felix, also known as Secretius, who lived 40 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900191.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sextus Mallius Messallinus lived for 30 years. Caecilia Quintula to her sweetest son.
EDCS-12900191.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caecilia Quintula to her beloved son, Sextus Mallius Messallinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900192.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mavia, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-12900192.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mavia, who lived 30 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900193.translation: Publius Manilius Maximus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 30 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900193.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Manilius Maximus, identifying him by his father and tribe, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900194.translation: Publius Manilius Severus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 52 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Manilius Severus, identifying him by his father and tribe, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900195.translation: To the Divine Manes. Manilia Gargilia.
EDCS-12900195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manilia Gargilia, likely incomplete, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900196.translation: To the Divine Manes. Manilia Nercorosa lived for 35 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900196.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manilia Nercorosa, wishing for her peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900197.translation: To the Divine Manes. Manilia Vitalis lived for 9 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900197.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manilia Vitalis, who died at the young age of nine, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900198.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Luc[i]a Manlia Honorata lived for 30 years.
EDCS-12900198.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the death of Lucia Manlia Honorata, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-12900199.translation: Marcella lived for 15 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcella, who lived 15 years and is buried here, with a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900200.translation: To the memory of Marcellina. Trophimus [made this] for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-12900200.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Marcellina by Trophimus for his beloved wife.

EDCS-12900201.translation: [---] Geminius(?) Marcianus lived for 7 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900201.summary: This is a funerary inscription for [---] Geminius Marcianus, who lived 7 years, with a traditional wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900203.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Quintus Marcius Fortis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, who lived for 75 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900203.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Quintus Marcius Fortis, a man of the Quirina tribe who lived 75 years, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900204.translation: Aulus Marcius Iucundus, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 14 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Marcius Iucundus, a youth of the Quirina tribe who lived 14 years, indicating his burial place and wishing his bones peace.

EDCS-12900206.translation: Marcia Tertulla, daughter of Gaius, lived for 30 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcia Tertulla, daughter of Gaius, who lived 30 years, marking her burial place and including a wish for her bones.

EDCS-12900207.translation: Gaius Marius lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900207.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Gaius Marius, stating he lived 50 years and is buried in this location.

EDCS-12900210.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Martialis lived for 6 years.
EDCS-12900210.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Martialis, a child who lived for 6 years.

EDCS-12900211.translation: Martialis lived for 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Martialis, stating he lived 15 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-12900213.translation: Publius Mellisaeus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Bassus, lived 22 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900213.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Mellisaeus Bassus, placed by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-12900214.translation: To the Divine Manes. Memmius Severianus lived 4 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Memmius Severianus, a young child, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900215.translation: Lucius Memmius Oceanus lived 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900215.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Memmius Oceanus, likely from the Imperial period, erected by an unspecified dedicant.

EDCS-12900216.translation: To the Divine Manes. Meridianus lived 50 years.
EDCS-12900216.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Meridianus, likely from the Imperial period, with the final resting place not explicitly stated.

EDCS-12900217.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Minucius Victor lived 55 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900217.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Marcus Minucius Victor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900219.translation: Lucius Modius Nicephor lived 100 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Modius Nicephor, notable for his advanced age, including a common valedictory wish, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900220.translation: To the Divine Manes. Modia Hospita lived 29 years. Celius Viator lived 5 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900220.summary: This is a shared funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Modia Hospita and Celius Viator, likely mother and child or close relatives, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900221.translation: Monnosa lived 15 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900221.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Monnosa, a young woman, likely from the Imperial period, with an unspecified dedicant.

EDCS-12900222.translation: To the Divine Manes. Munatia Secunda lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900222.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Munatia Secunda, likely from the Imperial period, with a partial indication of her burial.

EDCS-12900223.translation: To the Divine Manes. Munatia Secundilla lived...
EDCS-12900223.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Munatia Secundilla, indicating a young woman who died at an unknown age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900224.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Mundicius Saturninus lived 95 years; he is buried here, may your bones rest well. To the Divine Manes. Umbria Matronica, I was a ripening of humans. From me was long servitude of fear for this deity and of religion, which I served for 80 years, even barefoot, chastely and modestly and earnestly I appeared to the cities of the whole earth, and therefore I received such merits from her, that the earth might kindly receive me. She lived 115 years; she is buried here, may your bones rest well. Matronica.
EDCS-12900224.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording two individuals, Marcus Mundicius Saturninus and Umbria Matronica, with Matronica's epitaph detailing her long life and devoted service to a deity, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900225.translation: Munnia Reducta lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900225.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Munnia Reducta, who died at the age of 20, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900226.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sextus Murtius Muricelus lived 55 years.
EDCS-12900226.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Sextus Murtius Muricelus, who lived 55 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900228.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mustia lived 25 years; may she rest well here.
EDCS-12900228.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Mustia, who lived 25 years, expressing a wish for her peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900230.translation: Namp[hanus?] lived 10 years; he is buried here, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Namp[hanus?], a child who lived 10 years, with a standard wish for his bones to rest well, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900231.translation: To the Divine Manes. Nampulosa, a most sweet wife, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900231.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Nampulosa, described as a most sweet wife, who lived 25 years, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900232.translation: To the Divine Manes. Nicon, a slave of our Augusti, lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900232.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Nicon, a slave of the emperors, who died at the age of 21, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900234.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Novellus, who lived 41 years, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900235.translation: Quintulus Numisius Arator lived 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900235.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintulus Numisius Arator, who died at the age of 16, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900236.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Numisius M[---]ius lived [---] years; he is buried here. To the Divine Manes. Cacilia Novelia lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900236.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Quintus Numisius and Cacilia Novelia, who lived 40 years, with the age of Quintus being illegible, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900237.translation: To the Divine Manes. Numisia Marta lived 47 years. She is buried here. Sodalis (dedicated this) to his worthy wife.
EDCS-12900237.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by a man named Sodalis to his deceased wife, Numisia Marta, who died at the age of 47.

EDCS-12900238.translation: To the memory of Numisia Quadratilla.
EDCS-12900238.summary: This short inscription is a memorial dedicated to a woman named Numisia Quadratilla.

EDCS-12900240.translation: Sextus Octavius Serenus lived 4 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900240.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Sextus Octavius Serenus, who died at the age of four, and includes a common valediction for the deceased.

EDCS-12900241.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Octavius Venerius lived 47 years, but he lived. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900241.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Octavius Venerius, who lived for 47 years, emphasizing that he truly lived despite his death.

EDCS-12900242.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Octavia Sperata, daughter, she lived X[3] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia Sperata, identified as a daughter, whose age at death is partially preserved.

EDCS-12900243.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aisa Olimpias lived 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900243.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aisa Olimpias, who lived for forty years.

EDCS-12900245.translation: Orcivius Pultarius lived 11 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900245.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Orcivius Pultarius, who died at the age of eleven.

EDCS-12900246.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ovinia Marcellina, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years.
EDCS-12900246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ovinia Marcellina, identified as the daughter of Lucius and belonging to the Quirina tribe, who died at twenty-three years of age.

EDCS-12900247.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gnaeus P[...] Felix lived 75 years.
EDCS-12900247.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus P[...] Felix, who lived to the age of seventy-five.

EDCS-12900248.translation: To P[...] Herma, he lived 58 years, to a most dear father.
EDCS-12900248.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to P[...] Herma by a loved one, honoring him as a most dear father who lived for fifty-eight years.

EDCS-12900249.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pacia Optata lived 50 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900249.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pacia Optata, indicating she lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-12900250.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Pactumeia Hagne, an incomparable woman, most loving of her husband, lived 20 years. She lies here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-12900250.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pactumeia Hagne, erected by her husband, commemorating her life of 20 years and her loving nature.

EDCS-12900251.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pallusia Felicia lived 35 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900251.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pallusia Felicia, noting she lived for 35 years.

EDCS-12900252.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Passius Saturus lived 80 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900252.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Passius Saturus, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-12900253.translation: To the Divine Manes. Passidinius lived [---] years. Passidinia Pa[---] placed this for her most rare husband.
EDCS-12900253.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Passidinia to her husband, Passidinius, described as a 'most rare husband'.

EDCS-12900254.summary: This appears to be a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Peculiaris.

EDCS-12900255.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Petronius, [---] from the Quirina tribe, [---].
EDCS-12900255.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Petronius, noting his tribe as Quirina.

EDCS-12900257.translation: Gaius Petronius Pudens, son of Lucius, from the Quirina tribe, lived 17 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900257.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Petronius Pudens, son of Lucius and from the Quirina tribe, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-12900258.translation: Publius Petronius Restitutus, son of Lucius, from the Quirina tribe, lived 10 years. He lies here. He rests.
EDCS-12900258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Petronius Restitutus, son of Lucius and from the Quirina tribe, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-12900260.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pinarius Felix lived 40 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900260.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pinarius Felix, indicating he lived for 40 years.

EDCS-12900261.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pius Sittianus, he lived 71 years.
EDCS-12900261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pius Sittianus, indicating his age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900262.translation: Marcus Pomirius Felix lived 40 years. May he rest here, interred.
EDCS-12900262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pomirius Felix, noting his age and requesting peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900264.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Pompeius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe...
EDCS-12900264.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Pompeius, son of Gaius and a member of the Quirina tribe, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900265.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Quintus Pompeius Caecilianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900266.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pompeius Euticus lived 75 years.
EDCS-12900266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius Euticus, stating his age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900269.translation: Marcus Pompeius Primus, freedman of Marcus, lived 90 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-12900269.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pompeius Primus, a freedman of Marcus, noting his advanced age and burial place, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900270.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Pompeius Restutus lived 16 months. He is interred here.
EDCS-12900270.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Pompeius Restutus, who died as an infant at 16 months old, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900271.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Pompeius Sabinianus lived 100 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-12900271.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pompeius Sabinianus, recording his exceptional age of 100 years at death, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900272.translation: Pompeius, a slave born in the household, lived 84 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-12900272.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius, a 'verna' (household slave), indicating his age and burial location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900273.translation: Pompeius IPITI(?), lived 35 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-12900273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius, whose cognomen is partially illegible, stating his age at death and burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900274.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pompeius Pecu[nentus] lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius Pecu[nentus], likely erected by his family or associates, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900275.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Pompeia Leontis, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-12900275.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pompeia Leontis, likely placed by her family or community, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900276.translation: [P]ompeia Nata lived 11 years. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-12900276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeia Nata, a young girl, probably erected by her parents, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Pomponius Macer, likely commemorating his death, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900279.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Pomponius Martialis, son of Lucius, lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pomponius Martialis, son of Lucius, likely erected by his family, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900280.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pontia Extricatula lived 30 years.
EDCS-12900280.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pontia Extricatula, likely placed by her family or friends, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900282.translation: To the Divine Manes. Popilia Fortuna Munula, pious, lived 15 years.
EDCS-12900282.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Popilia Fortuna Munula, a young woman, highlighting her piety, likely erected by her family, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900283.translation: Lucius Postumius Postuminus, son of Lucius, lived 49 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900283.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Postumius Postuminus, son of Lucius, commemorating his life and burial, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900284.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Quintus F., recording his age at death, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900285.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Praepusa, freedwoman of our Augustus, a most rare, obedient, and chaste wife, with great piety, lived 35 years. She is buried here. Onesimus [erected this] for his wife.
EDCS-12900285.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Onesimus to his wife Praepusa, a freedwoman of the emperor, praising her virtues, dating from the Imperial period, likely 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12900287.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Propertia Flora lived 17 years.
EDCS-12900287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Propertia Flora, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-12900288.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Propertia Monnula, daughter of Quintus, lived 25 years.
EDCS-12900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Propertia Monnula, the daughter of Quintus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-12900289.translation: Propertia Rogata, daughter of Decimus, lived 103 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900289.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Propertia Rogata, daughter of Decimus, who lived an exceptional 103 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900290.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publicia Veneria lived 5 years.
EDCS-12900290.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Veneria, who died at the young age of 5.

EDCS-12900291.translation: To Lucius Publius Marianus, he lived 20 years.
EDCS-12900291.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Publius Marianus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-12900292.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Publicius Barbarus, son of Fusca, he lived 10 years and 1 month.
EDCS-12900292.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicius Barbarus, the son of Fusca, who died at the age of 10 years and 1 month.

EDCS-12900293.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Publicius Fuscus lived 3 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900293.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Publicius Fuscus, who died at the age of 3 and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900295.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Publicius Vindex, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, an innocent son, lived 3 years.
EDCS-12900295.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Publicius Vindex, an innocent son of Marcus from the Quirina tribe, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-12900296.translation: Publicia Contenta, daughter of Gaius, lived 55 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900296.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Contenta, daughter of Gaius, who lived 55 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900297.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Monna, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-12900298.translation: Publicia Rogata, daughter of Aulus, lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900298.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Rogata, erected by an unspecified dedicant, likely a family member, commemorating her life.

EDCS-12900299.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Pullius Avius lived 15 years.
EDCS-12900299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Gaius Pullius Avius who died at the age of fifteen.

EDCS-12900300.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pupia Data lived 70 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900300.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Pupia Data who lived to the age of seventy.

EDCS-12900303.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus, slave of Gaius Apronius A., lived 40 years.
EDCS-12900303.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus, a slave of Gaius Apronius, who lived for 40 years, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900304.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quinta Germanilla lived 25 years.
EDCS-12900304.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Quinta Germanilla who died at the age of twenty-five.

EDCS-12900305.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintia Valeria lived 60 years.
EDCS-12900305.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Quintia Valeria who lived for sixty years.

EDCS-12900307.translation: To the Divine Manes. Roccia Matrona, a most sweet daughter, lived one year and three months. Roccius Homullus, her father, lies here.
EDCS-12900307.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a father, Roccius Homullus, for his beloved daughter, Roccia Matrona, who died in infancy, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900308.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Rocta Poppia, who lived to be 30 years old, with some uncertainty in the name transcription.

EDCS-12900309.translation: Gnaeus Romanius Longus, son of Gnaeus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900309.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gnaeus Romanius Longus, who lived to an old age of ninety years, mentioning his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12900310.translation: To the Divine Manes. Romanus lived 21 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900310.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Romanus who died at the age of twenty-one.

EDCS-12900311.translation: Sextus Roscius Catulus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900311.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Roscius Catulus, indicating his age and wishing him well in the afterlife.

EDCS-12900313.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Sabellius Dilectus lived 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900313.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Gaius Sabellius Dilectus, noting his long life and burial place.

EDCS-12900314.translation: To the memory of Lucius Sacricus Victor, who lived 51 years. Victoria, his daughter, placed this for her dearest father.
EDCS-12900314.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Victoria for her beloved father, Lucius Sacricus Victor, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12900315.translation: Publius Saenius Malminus lived 27 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900315.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Saenius Malminus, recording his age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900317.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sallustsi(?) MONLIM(?) lived 45 years. He is buried here (?).
EDCS-12900317.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the spirits of the dead for Sallustsi(?) MONLIM(?), noting their age and probable burial place, though some details are uncertain due to illegibility.

EDCS-12900319.translation: To the Divine Manes. Salvidienius Fidianus lived 32 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900319.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Salvidienius Fidianus, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-12900320.translation: For Gaius Salvidienus Lupus, he lived 15 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Salvidienus Lupus, recording his young age at death and his burial place.

EDCS-12900321.translation: Quintus Salvidienus Saturninus lived 11 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900321.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Salvidienus Saturninus, noting his young age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900322.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Salvidenia Tina.
EDCS-12900322.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the dead for Salvidenia Tina.

EDCS-12900323.translation: To the Divine Manes. Salvidenia Minna, daughter of Quintus, a woman of ancient chastity, lived 28 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900323.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the spirits of the dead for Salvidenia Minna, a woman praised for her chastity, recording her age and wishing her well in the afterlife.

EDCS-12900324.translation: Publius Salvinius Arator, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 47 years. He is laid here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-12900324.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Salvinius Arator, erected by an unnamed dedicator, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-12900325.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Of Samiarius.
EDCS-12900325.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Samiarius, common in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-12900326.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sarnia Saturnina lived for [...] years.
EDCS-12900326.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sarnia Saturnina, with her age missing from the surviving text, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12900327.translation: Sasa lived 22 years. She is laid here. May your bones rest well. Cornelius AIVO(?).
EDCS-12900327.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sasa, mentioning her age and a partially preserved name, Cornelius, likely the dedicator, typical of Roman epitaphs.

EDCS-12900330.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Satria AIE(?) lived 13 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-12900330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Satria, recording her age, with a partly illegible surname, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12900332.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Scruvia, stating her age, characteristic of many Roman gravestones.

EDCS-12900333.translation: Quintus Sedius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-12900333.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sedius Celer, recording his age and tribal affiliation, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-12900334.translation: Marcus Sedius Severus lived 35 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-12900334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Sedius Severus, stating his age, typical of Roman tombstone texts.

EDCS-12900335.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Seia Agathopula lived 33 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-12900335.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Seia Agathopula, recording her age, common in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12900336.translation: Victorinus made this for Seia Concordia, his wife.
EDCS-12900336.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Victorinus dedicates a monument to his wife, Seia Concordia, illustrating marital devotion in Roman society.

EDCS-12900337.translation: Seia Lucia, daughter of Lucius, lived 100 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900337.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Seia Lucia, a woman who lived to a remarkable age.

EDCS-12900339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sellesius Nampamo (?), a young individual who lived fifteen years.

EDCS-12900340.translation: Sempronia Extricata lived 58 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900340.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronia Extricata, commemorating her life of 58 years.

EDCS-12900341.translation: To the Divine Manes. Senia Optata lived 59 years. Lucius Insieius Vitalis [made this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-12900341.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Insieius Vitalis to his deserving wife, Senia Optata, who lived for 59 years.

EDCS-12900342.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aufidius (?) Serenus, most devoted brother (?). [---]
EDCS-12900342.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Aufidius (?) Serenus, possibly by a brother, expressing profound devotion.

EDCS-12900343.translation: To the Divine Manes. Septimius Coddeus [---]
EDCS-12900343.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Septimius Coddeus.

EDCS-12900344.translation: Sergius Socrates [made this] for Sergia Pudentiana, his freedwoman. The freedman lived 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12900344.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Sergius Socrates for his freedwoman, Sergia Pudentiana, also recording his own life span of 50 years and burial place.

EDCS-12900345.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Sergius Sozonus lived 55 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12900345.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Sergius Sozonus, recording his age of 55 years at death.

EDCS-12900346.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sergia Sodala lived 31 years. She lies here.
EDCS-12900346.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sergia Sodala, who lived 31 years.

EDCS-12900347.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Srvandus, a young individual who lived fifteen years.

EDCS-12900348.translation: Servilia Lucia lived for 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900348.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial of Servilia Lucia.

EDCS-12900349.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aulus Sextius Urbanus lived for 100 years.
EDCS-12900349.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Aulus Sextius Urbanus, who lived to an old age.

EDCS-12900350.translation: Sextia Iulia, daughter of Saturus, lived for 81 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextia Iulia, daughter of Saturus, noting her age and burial location.

EDCS-12900351.translation: To the Divine Manes. S. Faba lived for 5 years.
EDCS-12900351.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for S. Faba, a child who lived only five years.

EDCS-12900352.translation: Lucius Silius Extricatus lived for 10 years and 3 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900352.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Silius Extricatus, detailing his short life span and burial.

EDCS-12900353.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for someone named Sitacus, stating their age at death.

EDCS-12900354.translation: Sitillia Gudula, freedwoman of Titus, lived for 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900354.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sitillia Gudula, a freedwoman, indicating her age and burial spot.

EDCS-12900355.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Sittius, son of A. M., lived for 40 years. He is here.
EDCS-12900355.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Marcus Sittius, including his age and presence at the site.

EDCS-12900356.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Sittius Abascantio lived for 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900356.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Marcus Sittius Abascantio, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-12900357.translation: Lucius Sittius Acceptus, freedman of Lucius, lived for 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900357.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Acceptus, a freedman, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-12900358.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of Gaius Sittius Antistianus, likely part of a larger monument where the rest of the text is lost.

EDCS-12900359.translation: To the memory of Lucius Sittius Augustalis, a lover of his suburban region Azimacianum, which he built from the ground up for himself and his family. He made it with good things, well.
EDCS-12900359.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Sittius Augustalis to his own memory, commemorating his construction of a suburban property called Azimacianum for himself and his family.

EDCS-12900360.translation: Publius Sittius Consentus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900360.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Consentus, who lived for 70 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900361.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sittius Crescens, lived 50 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900361.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittius Crescens, who lived for 50 years, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900362.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Sittius Dativus lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900362.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Dativus, recording his death at the age of 30 and his burial at this site.

EDCS-12900363.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sittius Extricatus lived 67 years.
EDCS-12900363.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Sittius Extricatus, stating he lived for 67 years, likely indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-12900364.translation: Lucius Sittius Fabarius lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900364.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Fabarius, noting he lived for 60 years and is interred at this spot.

EDCS-12900365.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Sittius Felix lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900365.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Felix, indicating he lived for 75 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900366.translation: Lucius Sittius Fundanus, son of Lucius, lived 7 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900366.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Fundanus, son of Lucius, who died at the young age of 7 and is buried here.

EDCS-12900367.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Sittius Hor[...], Augu[...], lived [...] years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900367.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Quintus Sittius, with parts of his name and age missing, concluding with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12900368.translation: Lucius Sittius Ianuaris lived 19 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900368.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Ianuaris, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-12900369.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Sittius Liberalis is buried here. He lived 43 years.
EDCS-12900369.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Publius Sittius Liberalis and his age at death.

EDCS-12900370.translation: Publius Sittius Lucullus Iamgur, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 7 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900370.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Lucullus Iamgur, indicating his parentage, tribe, and young age at death, and offering a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900371.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Sittius Marcellus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-12900373.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Sittius Martialis lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900373.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Lucius Sittius Martialis and his age at death.

EDCS-12900374.translation: Lucius Sittius Maxsimus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 61 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900374.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Maxsimus, noting his parentage, tribe, age, and burial place.

EDCS-12900375.translation: Lucius Sittius Nerva, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900375.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Nerva, recording his parentage, tribe, venerable age, and burial place.

EDCS-12900377.translation: Publius Sittius Paulinus lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900377.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Paulinus, indicating his age at death and burial place.

EDCS-12900378.translation: Publius Sittius Philetus lived 45 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900378.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Philetus, recording his age and burial, and including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900379.translation: To Sittius Rapax. He lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900379.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittius Rapax, stating his age at death and burial place.

EDCS-12900380.translation: Sittius Saturninus lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900380.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial of Sittius Saturninus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900381.translation: To the memory of Publius Sittius Tullianus. Caecilia Ianuaria, his dearest wife, with their children, made this for her most loving husband. He lived 65 years.
EDCS-12900381.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Caecilia Ianuaria and her children to her most beloved husband, Publius Sittius Tullianus, who lived 65 years, likely dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-12900382.translation: Sittia, daughter of Publius, lived [approximately] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900382.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Sittia, daughter of Publius, recording her age and burial, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900384.translation: Sittia Augurina, daughter of Publius, lived 6 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900384.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Augurina, daughter of Publius, who died at the young age of six, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900385.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sittia Bona lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900385.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the name, age, and burial of Sittia Bona, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900386.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Sittia Credula.
EDCS-12900386.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Sittia Credula, likely part of a larger monument from the Imperial period, though the full context of the dedication is missing.

EDCS-12900387.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sittia Danae lived 11 years.
EDCS-12900387.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Sittia Danae, who died at the age of eleven, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900388.translation: Sittia Fabucia, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900388.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Fabucia, daughter of Lucius, who lived 25 years, indicating her burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900389.translation: Sittia Faustilla lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900389.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial of Sittia Faustilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900390.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Sittia Felicitas, her dearest mother, she lived 71 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900390.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Sittia Felicitas, a mother who lived 71 years, likely dedicated by her children and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900392.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sittia Honorata lived 50 years.
EDCS-12900392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Honorata, recording her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900394.translation: Sittia Hospita, daughter of Publius, lived 50 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900394.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Hospita, daughter of Publius, indicating her age and burial place.

EDCS-12900395.translation: Sittia Ingenua, daughter of Lucius, lived 66 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900395.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Ingenua, daughter of Lucius, stating her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900396.translation: Sittia Marselina, daughter of Publius, lived 33 years.
EDCS-12900396.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Marselina, daughter of Publius, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12900397.translation: Sittia Maxima, daughter of Publius, lived 16 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900397.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Maxima, daughter of Publius, commemorating her short life and including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-12900398.translation: Sittia Modesta, daughter of Publius, lived 31 years.
EDCS-12900398.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Modesta, daughter of Publius, noting her age at death.

EDCS-12900399.translation: Sittia Monna, daughter of Publius, lived 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900399.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Monna, daughter of Publius, indicating her age and burial location.

EDCS-12900400.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sittia Mustia lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900400.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for Sittia Mustia, stating her age and a blessing for her remains.

EDCS-12900401.translation: Sittia Mustia lived 40 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900401.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Mustia, recording her age at death and indicating her burial spot.

EDCS-12900402.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sittia Natalis lived 70 years.
EDCS-12900402.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for Sittia Natalis, noting her age at death.

EDCS-12900403.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sittia Nina lived 101 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900403.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Nina, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Rogata, stating her age at death.

EDCS-12900405.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Sittia Rufina Sapi, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-12900405.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Rufina Sapi, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-12900406.translation: Sittia Samate, daughter of Quintus, lived 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900406.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Samate, indicating her father's name, age, and burial place.

EDCS-12900407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Saturnina, stating her age at death.

EDCS-12900408.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Sittia Stercula, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-12900408.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Stercula, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12900409.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sitta Successa lived 56 years; she is here, may she rest well.
EDCS-12900409.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sitta Successa, recording her age and including a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900410.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Sittia Valerilla, who lived 20 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900410.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Valerilla, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900411.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Sittia Victoriana, who lived 81 years.
EDCS-12900411.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Victoriana, recording her advanced age at death.

EDCS-12900412.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sittia Urbanilla lived 21 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12900412.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Urbanilla, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-12900413.translation: To Sossius, to his son Eros.
EDCS-12900413.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of a father, Sossius, and his son, Eros.

EDCS-12900414.translation: Squama lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900414.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Squama, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-12900415.translation: Stabularia lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Stabularia, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-12900416.translation: Stacola(?), daughter of Gaius. Laila lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900416.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laila, daughter of Gaius, possibly also named Stacola, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-12900417.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Stephanus, a home-born slave of our Augustus, lived 5 years. He is here.
EDCS-12900417.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Stephanus, a young imperial slave who died at the age of five.

EDCS-12900418.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sufia Quitiosa(?) lived 35 years.
EDCS-12900418.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Sufia Quitiosa(?), who lived 35 years.

EDCS-12900419.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tanonia Mustia lived 101 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900419.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Tanonia Mustia, who lived to the remarkable age of 101.

EDCS-12900420.translation: Tarquinia Ingenua, daughter of Publius, lived 65 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900420.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tarquinia Ingenua, daughter of Publius, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12900421.translation: To the Divine Manes. Terentia Lucida's grandchild, Iucundula, lived 26 years. She is buried here. Gaius Iulius Martialis (erected this) for his wife.
EDCS-12900421.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Iucundula, granddaughter of Terentia Lucida, who lived 26 years, erected by her husband Gaius Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-12900422.translation: To the Divine Manes. Themis lived 31 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900422.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Themis, who lived 31 years.

EDCS-12900423.translation: ---ius, freedman of Lucius, Thiasus, lived 40 years. He is buried here. V(?).
EDCS-12900423.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Thiasus, a freedman of Lucius, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-12900424.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titinius Rogatus lived 101 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900424.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Titinius Rogatus, who lived to the age of 101.

EDCS-12900426.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Titius Martialis lived 7 years. He is buried here. // To the Divine Manes. Lucius Titius Ren[...], lived [...] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900426.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely for two members of the Titius family: Lucius Titius Martialis, who died at age seven, and another Lucius Titius, whose name and age are partially lost.

EDCS-12900427.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titia Vitalis, mother of sons, lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900427.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Titia Vitalis, described as a mother of sons, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-12900428.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Trasius Fatalis lived 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Trasius Fatalis, who lived 50 years, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900429.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Trebius Modestus, [...], lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900429.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Publius Trebius Modestus, who lived 20 years, with an unclear familial relation.

EDCS-12900430.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life of Quintus Tresius Nampamo, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-12900431.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Iulius Seianus lived 28 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900431.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Seianus, who lived 28 years, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12900432.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Tulius Severus, who lived 17 years.

EDCS-12900434.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Ceparius lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900434.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Valerius Ceparius, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-12900435.translation: To Valeria Concordia, who lived for 80 years.
EDCS-12900435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Concordia, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900436.translation: Valeria Donatula placed this for her granddaughter Iulia Quieta, the sweetest daughter of Quetus, who lived for 2 years.
EDCS-12900436.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Valeria Donatula for her young granddaughter, Iulia Quieta, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900437.translation: Valeria Helpis, who lived for 30 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-12900437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Helpis, including a common valediction, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900438.translation: Valeria Quinta, daughter of Quintus, lived for 10 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-12900438.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Valeria Quinta, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900439.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Urbanica, who lived for 41 years.
EDCS-12900439.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Valeria Urbanica, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900440.translation: Valgia Prima, who lived for 85 years, is buried here.
EDCS-12900440.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valgia Prima, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900442.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Vettius Quadratus Iunior lived for 8 years.
EDCS-12900442.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for a young boy, Gaius Vettius Quadratus Iunior, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900443.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vettius Victor lived for [unknown number] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900443.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Vettius Victor, with the age of death being illegible, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900444.translation: Vettia Baline, daughter of Sextus, lived for 24 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900444.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vettia Baline, erected by an unknown dedicant, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900445.translation: Marcus Vibius Castus lived for 75 years. He is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-12900445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Vibius Castus, including a common valediction, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900446.translation: Gaius Vibius Hermes lived 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900446.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vibius Hermes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900447.translation: Quintus Vibius Hilario lived 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Vibius Hilario, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900448.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Vibius Sabinus lived 50 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900448.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Lucius Vibius Sabinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900449.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibia Uranis lived 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900449.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Vibia Uranis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900450.translation: Vibia Vicana lived 12 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vibia Vicana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900451.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for a man named Victor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900452.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for a young man named Victor, son of an individual whose name is mostly lost, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900453.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription dedicated to the memory of Victorinus, likely a funerary monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900454.translation: [---]cia Victrix lived 70 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900454.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for a woman named Victrix, whose full nomen is lost, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900455.translation: Viriaria Nonis lived 65 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900455.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Viriaria Nonis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900456.translation: To the memory of Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, the most innocent father. Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus Iunior, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, his son, set this up.
EDCS-12900456.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus Iunior for his father, Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12900457.translation: [...] Vitalis, lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900457.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitalis, who lived 35 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900458.translation: Vitalis, 13 years old. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900458.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young person named Vitalis, aged 13, wishing their bones good rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900459.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Vitiaria Epictesis lived for [---] years.
EDCS-12900459.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Vitiaria Epictesis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900460.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vituvia Trophime lived 13 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900460.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vituvia Trophime, who lived 13 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900461.translation: To the memory of the Umbricia family. [Lucius Umb]ricius Rufinus Senior, son of Lucius Umbricius Rufus, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-12900461.summary: This is a family funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Umbricius Rufinus Senior to the memory of the Umbricius family and for himself and his descendants, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900462.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Primus lived 85 years.
EDCS-12900462.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Valerius Primus, who lived 85 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900463.translation: Quintus Vo[---] Prim[---] lived [---] years. LI[---]
EDCS-12900463.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a person named Quintus Vo[---] Prim[---], likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900464.translation: Volcatia Pacata lived 40 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900464.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volcatia Pacata, who lived 40 years, wishing her bones good rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900465.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Voltius Martialis lived 14 years and 2 months.
EDCS-12900465.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Marcus Voltius Martialis, who lived 14 years and 2 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12900468.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Volusius Felix.

EDCS-12900470.translation: To the Divine Manes. Urbanus, a sweet soul, lived 8 months, 8 days. He is laid here.
EDCS-12900470.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Urbanus, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900474.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Samiarius Galicius lived 16 years.
EDCS-12900474.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Samiarius Galicius, who lived sixteen years, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-12900477.translation: To the Divine Manes. Chodionius EC[---]
EDCS-12900477.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for someone named Chodionius.

EDCS-12900479.translation: Clara, daughter of Ucus, is laid here.
EDCS-12900479.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clara, indicating her father's name and her burial place.

EDCS-12900482.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Cae[---] Silanus lived [---] years.
EDCS-12900482.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Lucius Cae[---] Silanus, recording part of his name and age.

EDCS-12900485.translation: Sentia Africana, daughter of Lucius, lived 29 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-12900485.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sentia Africana, recording her name, filiation, age, and burial place.

EDCS-12900488.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Aulus Varonius Himnus.

EDCS-12900493.translation: To the Divine Manes. To the Divine Manes. Maritimus [---]lus lived 31 years.
EDCS-12900493.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Maritimus [---]lus, likely dedicated to the Divine Manes and recording his age.

EDCS-12900505.translation: [---]ia Pusinni [---] lived 35 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-12900505.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a woman named [---]ia Pusinni [---], recording her age and burial location.

EDCS-12900506.translation: ISAT(?) BELLA lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900506.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named ISAT BELLA, stating her age and burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000815.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Ulpius Absens lived 100 years. He is buried here. Ulpii Aprilis and Atimetus made this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-13000815.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Aprilis and Atimetus to their father, Marcus Ulpius Absens, who lived to an old age, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-13000816.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Ulpius Aprilis lived 26 years. He is buried here. Ulpius Atimetus made this for his devoted brother.
EDCS-13000816.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Atimetus to his brother, Marcus Ulpius Aprilis, who died at the age of 26, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-13000817.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ulpius Atimetus, recording his name and a portion of his age, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-13000952.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. The sons made a tomb for their father, Lucius Cominius (?) Rogatus, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-13000952.summary: This is a funerary inscription where sons dedicated a tomb (cupula) to their father, Lucius Cominius Rogatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000953.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. The sons made a tomb for their mother, Flavia Donata, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-13000953.summary: This is a funerary inscription where sons dedicated a tomb (cupula) to their mother, Flavia Donata, who died at the age of 85, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13001726.translation: Sacred to Bacax Augustus. On the 7th Ides of May, when Gentianus and Bassus were consuls, Gaius Iulius Frontonianus and Titus Seius Pudens, magistrates of the Thibilitani, made this.
EDCS-13001726.summary: This is a religious dedication to the deity Bacax Augustus, made by Gaius Iulius Frontonianus and Titus Seius Pudens, magistrates of Thibilis, on May 9th, 211 AD (based on the consuls Gentianus and Bassus).

EDCS-13001734.text_edition: Bacaci Augusto sacrum duobus Philippis consulibus Titus Iulius Victor Sorotius et Quintus Messius Felix maggistri Thibilitanorum
EDCS-13001734.translation: Sacred to Bacax Augustus. When the two Philippi were consuls, Titus Iulius Victor Sorotius and Quintus Messius Felix, magistrates of the Thibilitani, made this.
EDCS-13001734.summary: This is a religious dedication to the deity Bacax Augustus, made by Titus Iulius Victor Sorotius and Quintus Messius Felix, magistrates of Thibilis, in 247 AD (during the consulship of the two Philippi).

EDCS-13001747.translation: To Quintus Lollius Urbicus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, consul, Augustan legate of the province of Germania Inferior, fetial priest, legate of Emperor Hadrian in the Judean expedition, for which he was awarded a plain spear and a golden crown, legate of Legio X Gemina, praetor (candidate of Caesar), tribune of the plebs (candidate of Caesar), legate of the proconsul of Asia, quaestor of the city, military tribune (laticlavius) of Legio XXII Primigenia, one of the four commissioners for maintaining roads, patron. Dedicated by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-13001747.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription erected by public money (DD PP) in honor of Quintus Lollius Urbicus, detailing his extensive senatorial career and military achievements, including service under Hadrian in the Judean expedition, likely dating from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13001908.summary: This is a short inscription listing the names of two fortresses: Fussala and Caldis, likely a geographical or administrative note.

EDCS-13001994.text_edition: Veneri Augustae ndus legatus Augusti pro praetore dedit
EDCS-13001994.translation: ---ndus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, gave this to Venus Augusta.
EDCS-13001994.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Venus Augusta by a Roman legate named ---ndus, serving with propraetorian authority.
