EDCS-13002008.translation: Temple dedicated on the fifth day before the Kalends of March, which day was two days after the sixth day before the Kalends, when Lucius Venuleius Apronianus II and Lucius Sergius Paulus II were consuls.
EDCS-13002008.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a temple on February 25th in the year when Lucius Venuleius Apronianus (II) and Lucius Sergius Paulus (II) were consuls, corresponding to 169 AD.

EDCS-13002013.translation: He promised on the third day before the Nones of January, when Senecio and Aemilianus were consuls. He dedicated it on the nineteenth day before the Kalends of February, when the same men were consuls.
EDCS-13002013.summary: This inscription records the promise and subsequent dedication of a monument by an unnamed individual, occurring in January of the year when Senecio and Aemilianus were consuls (likely 236 AD).

EDCS-13002025.translation: To the Divine Pertinax, father of Augustus, Lucius Scantius Iulianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with a public horse, gave the statue he promised from the revenues of the amphitheater places on the day of the public spectacle which he exhibited from his generosity on account of the honor of the tresvirate.
EDCS-13002025.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to the deified Emperor Pertinax by Lucius Scantius Iulianus, who fulfilled a promise using funds from amphitheater revenues to celebrate his tresvirate.

EDCS-13002032.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Karino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Germanico maximo tribuniciae potestatis bis consuli patri patriae consuli proconsuli Marcus Aurelius Decimus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Numidiae ex principe peregrinorum devotus numini maiestatique eorum
EDCS-13002032.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus, Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, holding tribunician power twice, Consul, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul. Marcus Aurelius Decimus, most perfect equestrian, governor of the province of Numidia, formerly princeps peregrinorum, devoted to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-13002032.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Aurelius Decimus, governor of Numidia, to Emperor Carinus, praising his titles and expressing devotion.

EDCS-13002066.text_edition: Tito Caesernio Titi filio Palatina Statio Quintio Statiano Memmio Macrino consuli sodali Augustali legato pro praetore provinciae Africae legato legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis misso ad dilectum iuniorum a divo Hadriano in regionem Transpadanam tribuno plebis quaestori candidato divi Hadriani comiti eiusdem in Oriente XVvirum sic stlitibus iudicandis decreto decurionum patrono IIII coloniae pecunia publica
EDCS-13002066.translation: To Titus Caesernius Statio Quintius Statianus Memmius Macrinus, son of Titus, of the Palatina tribe, consul, Augustal sodalis, legate pro praetore of the province of Africa, legate of the Legio XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix, sent by the Divine Hadrian for the levy of youths in the Transpadane region, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, candidate of the Divine Hadrian, his companion in the East, quindecimvir for judging lawsuits. By decree of the decurions, as patron of the four colonies, with public money.
EDCS-13002066.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument, funded by public money and decreed by the decurions, to Titus Caesernius Statio Quintius Statianus Memmius Macrinus, detailing his extensive military, political, and priestly career, including service under Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-13002068.translation: To Marcus Claudius Restitutus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, procurator Augusti for the diocese of the Hadrumetine and Thevestine region and of the Matutinus games, and for auditing the accounts of Syrian cities, tribune of Legio VII Gemina, prefect of Cohort I Gaetulorum. Alexander, freedman of Augustus, archivist, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002068.summary: This inscription, erected by Alexander, a freedman and archivist, by decree of the decurions, honors Marcus Claudius Restitutus for his distinguished career as an imperial procurator, military tribune, and cohort prefect.

EDCS-13002079.text_edition: Publio Iulio Iuniano Martialiano clarissimo viro consuli quaestori provinciae Asiae tribuno plebei sic praetori curatori civitatis Calenorum curatori viarum Clodiae Cassiae et Ciminiae praefecto aerari militaris proconsuli provinciae Macedoniae legato legionis III Augustae Severianae Alexandrianae praesidi et patrono res publica Cirtensium decreto ordinis dedit dedicavitque
EDCS-13002079.translation: To Publius Iulius Iunianus Martialianus, most distinguished man, consul, quaestor of the province of Asia, tribune of the plebs, praetor, curator of the city of Cales, curator of the Clodia, Cassia, and Ciminia roads, prefect of the military treasury, proconsul of the province of Macedonia, legate of Legio III Augusta Severiana Alexandriana, governor and patron, the Republic of Cirtensium gave and dedicated this by decree of the order.
EDCS-13002079.summary: This inscription records the dedication by the Republic of Cirtensium, by decree of the local council, to Publius Iulius Iunianus Martialianus, a distinguished Roman official, listing his extensive senatorial and imperial appointments.

EDCS-13002080.text_edition: Publio Iulio Publi filio Quirina Geminio Marciano consuli sodali Titio proconsuli provinciae Macedoniae legato Auggustorum pro praetore provinciae Arabiae legato Auggustorum super vexillationes in Cappadocia legato Augusti legionis X geminae legato pro praetore provinciae Africae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori tribuno laticlavio legionis X Fretensis et legionis IIII Scythicae IIIviro kapitali optimo constantissimo Durmius Felix primipilaris legionis III Cyreneicae sic strator in Arabia maioris temporis legationis eius honoris causa dono dedit
EDCS-13002080.translation: To Publius Iulius Geminius Marcianus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, consul, sodalis Titius, proconsul of the province of Macedonia, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power in the province of Arabia, legate of the Augusti over vexillations in Cappadocia, legate of Augustus of Legio X Gemina, legate pro praetore of the province of Africa, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, laticlavian tribune of Legio X Fretensis and Legio IIII Scythica, tresvir capitalis, an excellent and most steadfast man. Durmius Felix, primipilaris of Legio III Cyreneica, also a strator in Arabia for a longer period of his legation, gave this as a gift for the sake of his honor.
EDCS-13002080.summary: This inscription is a honorary dedication by Durmius Felix, a primipilaris, to Publius Iulius Geminius Marcianus, detailing his distinguished senatorial career, including various military and provincial commands.

EDCS-13002091.translation: To Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, most distinguished boy, son of Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, most distinguished man, praetor, enrolled among tribunes, quaestor of the province of Baetica, Flavial-Titial priest, decorated by the judgment of our most sacred and strongest Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, legate sent by the most ample senate to the same Emperor in Germany and to Antoninus Caesar, designated Emperor, in Pannonia. Gaius Volumnius Marcellus Caecilianus, honored with a public horse, placed this out of due piety to his memory.
EDCS-13002091.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Gaius Volumnius Marcellus Caecilianus in memory of Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, a distinguished young man whose father had a notable political career and served as a legate under Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-13002103.translation: To the memory of Marcus Antonius Ianuarius, freedman of the emperor, assistant of the record office.
EDCS-13002103.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Antonius Ianuarius, an imperial freedman who worked in the record office, likely set up by family or colleagues.

EDCS-13002104.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aprilis, a native-born slave of our emperor, assistant of the record office, lived 20 years, 32 days. Thes[mus], heir, set this up from his own expense.
EDCS-13002104.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aprilis, an imperial slave who served as an assistant in the record office, set up by his heir, Thes[mus].

EDCS-13002105.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Publicius Namhamo, our sweetest father, who lived 65 years. His son, a clerk, and [his daughter-in-law ---] and [his grandchildren] Urbanica, Felicissima, Esmara, Coilia, made this for their sweetest grandfather.
EDCS-13002105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the children and grandchildren of Publicius Namhamo, a father and grandfather, with one son identified as a clerk.

EDCS-13002106.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Hilarus, foster-child of the advocate of the fiscus, lived 22 years.
EDCS-13002106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hilarus, a foster-child connected to an advocate of the imperial treasury, likely set up by his guardian or family.

EDCS-13002107.translation: Gaius Aufidius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina voting tribe, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Bracari in Judaea, military tribune of the Twelfth Legion Fulminata in Cappadocia, gave and dedicated a portico and private rooms for his pontificate, having contributed 10,000 sesterces to the public treasury as legitimate expenses.
EDCS-13002107.summary: This inscription records the donation and dedication of a portico and private rooms by Gaius Aufidius Maximus, a distinguished military officer, in honor of his pontificate, indicating a public benefaction and his civic and religious status.

EDCS-13002109.translation: Gaius Iulius Rufinus, son of Gaius, soldier of the Seventh Praetorian Cohort, in the century of Naevius, lived 22 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13002109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Rufinus, a soldier of the Praetorian Guard, detailing his name, unit, and age at death.

EDCS-13002110.translation: Quintus Rusticelius, of the Arnensis tribe, a soldier in the century of Quintus Vitulus, lies here.
EDCS-13002110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Rusticelius, a soldier, identifying his tribe and military unit.

EDCS-13002118.translation: Baebia Casta, daughter of Publicus, of the Quirina voting tribe, priestess of Juno, lived 54 years. She lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-13002118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia Casta, a priestess of Juno, indicating her religious role and family affiliation.

EDCS-13002126.translation: To the memory of Lucius Fabius Felix, aedile, quaestor, triumvir, having held all honors, triumvir of the four colonies, father of the Roman knights Rusticus Frontonius Severus and Fabia Monnula, lived 80 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13002126.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Lucius Fabius Felix, a man who held numerous political and civic offices and was the father of Roman knights, highlighting his distinguished career and family.

EDCS-13002128.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Gargilius Felix, priest of Saturn, lived 95 years. He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. He lies here.
EDCS-13002128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Gargilius Felix, a priest of Saturn, noting his age and devotion.

EDCS-13002130.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Fundanius Modestus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, duumvir.
EDCS-13002130.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Gaius Fundanius Modestus, a man who held the public office of duumvir, set up by an unspecified dedicant likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002133.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julia Postuma, priestess of Juno, lived for 1 year.
EDCS-13002133.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Postuma, a priestess of Juno who died at the young age of one year, indicating an infant or very young child held this religious title, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002134.translation: Quintus Junius Firminus, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, aedile, duumvir, quaestor, perpetual flamen, lived for 67 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002134.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commemorating Quintus Junius Firminus, detailing his extensive political and religious career including the offices of aedile, duumvir, quaestor, and perpetual flamen, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002135.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Pater, priest of Bellona, lived for 60 years.
EDCS-13002135.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Lucius Pater, a priest of the goddess Bellona, who lived to be sixty years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002138.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Pompeius Quintus, priest of Saturn, husband, lived for 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002138.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Pompeius Quintus, a priest of Saturn, mentioning his status as a husband, and indicating he lived to sixty-five years of age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002141.translation: Publius Sittius Dento, son of Publius, aedile, duumvir, quaestor for the second time, quinquennial flamen, lived for 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002141.summary: This is a funerary monument for Publius Sittius Dento, recording his significant public career, including roles as aedile, duumvir, quaestor, and quinquennial flamen, who lived to the age of sixty, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002173.translation: Publius Caecilius, merchant, lived for 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Caecilius, identifying him as a merchant who lived to the age of fifty-five, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002175.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Virius Ianuarius vixit LX
EDCS-13002175.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Virius Ianuarius lived for 60 years.
EDCS-13002175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits in honor of Lucius Virius Ianuarius, who lived to be sixty years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002194.text_edition: Dis Manibus Calpurni=Oa#Calpurnia#CALPUROA Quaetea vixit annis LV
EDCS-13002194.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Calpurnia Quaetea lived for 55 years.
EDCS-13002194.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Calpurnia Quaetea, noting she lived for fifty-five years, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002195.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Armeniaco Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII consuli III decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-13002195.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Armeniacus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the eighteenth time, consul for the third time. By decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-13002195.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Armeniacus Augustus, listing his full imperial titles and lineage, erected by decree of the decurions with public funds, and dates to 164 AD based on his tribunician power for the eighteenth time.

EDCS-13002196.text_edition: us Sexti filius Quirina Cicero arcum quem ob honorem magisterii aediliciae iuris dictionis promiserat ex HS III milibus fecit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-13002196.translation: Sextus' son, of the Quirina tribe, Cicero, built and dedicated the arch which he had promised for the honor of his aedilician magistracy and jurisdiction, at a cost of three thousand sesterces.
EDCS-13002196.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of an arch by Cicero, son of Sextus, in honor of his aedilician magistracy, built at a cost of 3,000 sesterces, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-13002197.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Bombia Rogata Tuti filia vixit annos CI hic sita est
EDCS-13002197.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Bombia Rogata, daughter of Tutus, lived 101 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13002197.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Bombia Rogata, daughter of Tutus, who lived to the age of 101, and marks her burial place.

EDCS-13002198.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caecilia ExtricaRta#Extricata#EXTRICRTA vixit annos LXI hic sita est
EDCS-13002198.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caecilia Extricata lived 61 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13002198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Extricata, noting she lived for 61 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13002199.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Iulius Saturninus vixit annos LXI
EDCS-13002199.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Iulius Saturninus lived 61 years.
EDCS-13002199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Saturninus, recording that he lived for 61 years.

EDCS-13002200.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Ianuaria vixit annos LI hic sita est
EDCS-13002200.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Ianuaria lived 51 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13002200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Ianuaria, stating she lived 51 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-13002202.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Sittius Rusticus vixit annos XXXXVII hic situs est
EDCS-13002202.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Sittius Rusticus lived 47 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13002202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Sittius Rusticus, indicating he lived for 47 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13002203.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Vitruvius Victor vixit annos XLIX hic situs est
EDCS-13002203.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Vitruvius Victor lived 49 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13002203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Vitruvius Victor, stating he lived for 49 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-13002204.text_edition: Mius Cerealis vixit annos VII hic situs est
EDCS-13002204.translation: Mius Cerealis lived 7 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13002204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mius Cerealis, a child who lived only 7 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13002215.translation: Quintus C[...]ius ... Promised on the Nones of January, and dedicated on the Nones of March, during the consulship of Sabinianus and Seleucus.
EDCS-13002215.summary: This inscription records the promise and subsequent dedication of an unknown monument by Quintus C[...]ius, occurring in the same year of the consulship of Sabinianus and Seleucus (likely 295 CE).

EDCS-13002236.translation: Marcus Fabius Fronto, augur and prefect with jurisdiction, gave, in addition to one thousand denarii for the work of the theater, with scenic games, in the name of his son Senecio. He promised on the Nones of January and dedicated on the day before the Kalends of April, during the consulship of Fuscus II and Dexter.
EDCS-13002236.summary: This inscription details Marcus Fabius Fronto, an augur and prefect, funding the construction of a theater and providing scenic games in the name of his son Senecio, with a promise made on January 5th and dedication on March 31st, during the consulship of Fuscus II and Dexter (likely 225 CE).

EDCS-13002487.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Of Ania Getula, she lived 16 years. GEPACIN.
EDCS-13002487.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ania Getula, who lived to be 16 years old, likely set up by family or friends.

EDCS-13002507.text_edition: Lari Libero Augusto sacrum Quintus Quadratius  filius Quirina Quintulus aediliciae potestatis statuam cum aedicula et columnis quam in praefectura sua pro IIIviris agens sponte pollicitus est praeter legitima HS XX nummum decurionatus rei publicae inlata dedit dedicavitque locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-13002507.translation: Sacred to Lar Liber Augustus. Quintus Quadratius Quintulus, son of Quirina, holding aedilician power, promised a statue with a shrine and columns during his prefecture, acting for the triumvirs. Beyond his legal obligations, he contributed 20 sesterces to the public treasury for his decurionatus and gave and dedicated it. The space was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002507.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue, shrine, and columns to Lar Liber Augustus by Quintus Quadratius Quintulus, fulfilling a promise made during his public service as an aedile and prefect, with the site approved by the town council.

EDCS-13002512.translation: Julia Pamilla, she lived 35 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-13002512.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Julia Pamilla, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-13002513.translation: Sittia Ianuaria, she lived 30 years, she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002513.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Ianuaria, noting her age at death and including a common wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-13002952.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Claudia Concordia, she lived 37 years, she is buried here. Amandus made this for his wife.
EDCS-13002952.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Amandus to his incomparable wife, Claudia Concordia, who lived for 37 years.

EDCS-13003259.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae apneboti sic imperatori II pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII consuli III cohors I Chalcidenorum equitata Caio Maesio Picatiano legato Augustorum pro praetore
EDCS-13003259.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Divus Antoninus, grandson of Divus Hadrianus, great-grandson of Divus Traianus Parthicus, great-great-grandson of Divus Nerva, Imperator for the second time, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the eighteenth time, Consul for the third time, the First Cohort of Chalcidenians, mounted, (set this up under) Gaius Maesius Picatianus, legatus Augustorum pro praetore.
EDCS-13003259.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the First Mounted Cohort of Chalcidenians to Emperor Caracalla, honoring his imperial titles and achievements, and mentioning their provincial governor.

EDCS-13003291.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucilia Terentia and their son Lucilianus made this for Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, procurator, an incomparable husband and a most beloved father.
EDCS-13003291.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lucilia Terentia and her son Lucilianus for Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, a procurator, honoring him as an incomparable husband and most beloved father.

EDCS-13003304.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aulus Caelius Cuminus, he lived 40 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13003304.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Caelius Cuminus, noting his age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-13003305.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Aebutius Publianus, he lived 10 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13003305.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aebutius Publianus, who died at the age of ten and is buried here.

EDCS-13003306.translation: To the memory of Aeliana, sweetest wife, and Leontis...
EDCS-13003306.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedicated to Aeliana, described as a most sweet wife, and possibly her child Leontis, though incomplete.

EDCS-13003307.translation: Aelia Bassa, lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003307.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Aelia Bassa at age 40.

EDCS-13003308.translation: Aemilius Iulius Tanusius, lived 20 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003308.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Iulius Tanusius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13003309.translation: Lucius Aeutius Bassus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 21 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aeutius Bassus, noting his lineage and tribe, who died at 21.

EDCS-13003310.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Aerenius Bassus, lived 40 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003310.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Lucius Aerenius Bassus, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-13003311.translation: To the Divine Manes. Alfius Victorinus, lived 11 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003311.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Alfius Victorinus, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-13003312.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Arruntius Anaxar[...
EDCS-13003312.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Arruntius Anaxar.

EDCS-13003313.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelia Euplia, a most sweet wife...
EDCS-13003313.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Aurelia Euplia, likely erected by her husband.

EDCS-13003314.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to the memory of an individual named Aurelius.

EDCS-13003316.translation: FINTAI(?) Blandia, lived [---] years. She lies here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blandia, including a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-13003317.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Bromius, lived 10 years. He lies here. Bacchus placed this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-13003317.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Bromius, who died at 10, erected by his father Bacchus.

EDCS-13003318.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus C. Crispinus lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003318.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus C. Crispinus, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-13003319.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caecilia Vitalis lived 21 years.
EDCS-13003319.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Vitalis, who lived for 21 years.

EDCS-13003320.translation: To the memory of Claudia Maxima, who lived 14 years, 2 months, and 4 days. Claudius Ceriolus made this for his most rare daughter.
EDCS-13003320.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Claudius Ceriolus to the memory of his daughter, Claudia Maxima, who died at the age of 14 years and 2 months.

EDCS-13003321.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cominia Matrona lived 101 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13003321.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cominia Matrona, who lived to the remarkable age of 101 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003322.translation: Publius Colosius Proculus lived 10 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003322.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Colosius Proculus, who died at the age of 10, wishing his remains a peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003323.translation: Marcus Cominius Romanus lived 50 years; he is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13003323.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cominius Romanus, who lived for 50 years and is buried at this site.

EDCS-13003324.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Cominius Proculus lived 35 years; he is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13003324.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cominius Proculus, who lived 35 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003326.translation: Cornelia Luculla lived 25 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13003326.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Luculla, who lived for 25 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13003327.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cornelia Secunda lived 31 years; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13003327.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Secunda, who lived for 31 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003328.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for a woman named Curia.

EDCS-13003329.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dativa lived 10 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-13003329.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dativa, a young girl who died at the age of ten, wishing her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003330.translation: To the Divine Manes. Domitia lived 35 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13003330.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Domitia, who passed away at thirty-five years old.

EDCS-13003331.translation: Gaius Fabius Aflanus lived 21 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13003331.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Fabius Aflanus, who lived for 21 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13003332.translation: To the Divine Manes. Of Faustillus, who lived 39 years.
EDCS-13003332.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Faustillus, recording his age at death as thirty-nine years.

EDCS-13003334.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Ianuaria lived 4 years.
EDCS-13003334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Ianuaria, who died as a young child at the age of four.

EDCS-13003335.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Furnia Frontilla, who lived 4 years. Haelvia Maximilla placed this for her daughter.
EDCS-13003335.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Haelvia Maximilla for her daughter, Furnia Frontilla, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-13003336.translation: Sextus Gargilius Oriculo lived 90 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13003336.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Gargilius Oriculo, noting his advanced age of ninety years at his passing.

EDCS-13003338.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Orcivius Pusincinus lived 71 years; he is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Manes. Orchivia Urbana lived [---] years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13003338.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, dedicating monuments to Gaius Orcivius Pusincinus, who lived 71 years, and to Orchivia Urbana, whose age at death is partly lost.

EDCS-13003339.translation: Aulus Granius Lucullus lived 85 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13003339.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Aulus Granius Lucullus, recording that he lived 85 years and is interred at this site.

EDCS-13003340.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Rania Meetine lived 30 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13003340.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Rania Meetine, who passed away at the age of thirty and is buried here.

EDCS-13003341.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Grania Saturnina lived for 4 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003341.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Grania Saturnina, erected by an unnamed dedicant, likely a family member, commemorating her short life.

EDCS-13003342.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Chordionius Aelianus lived for 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003342.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Chordionius Aelianus, expressing a wish for peaceful rest for the deceased.

EDCS-13003343.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Chordionius Epaphroditus lived for 85 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003343.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Chordionius Epaphroditus, erected by an unnamed dedicant, commemorating his long life.

EDCS-13003344.translation: To the memory of Hordionius Exuperans, who lived for 3 years. His father made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-13003344.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a father for his beloved young son, Hordionius Exuperans, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-13003345.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Hospes and Gaius Iulius Gentius placed this for their most unique brother. He lived for 23 years.
EDCS-13003345.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Hospes and Gaius Iulius Gentius to their brother, who lived for 23 years.

EDCS-13003346.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Longinus lived for 80 years.
EDCS-13003346.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Longinus, erected by an unnamed dedicant, noting his age at death.

EDCS-13003347.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Maximus lived for 25 years. He lies here, may he rest.
EDCS-13003347.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Maximus, expressing a wish for his peaceful rest, erected by an unnamed dedicant.

EDCS-13003348.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Namphamo lived for 60 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003348.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Namphamo, erected by an unnamed dedicant, commemorating his life.

EDCS-13003349.translation: Decimus Iulius Palma, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 10 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003349.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decimus Iulius Palma, a boy who lived for 10 years, noting his father's name and tribe.

EDCS-13003350.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius [G]entianus lived for 16 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Gentianus, erected by an unnamed dedicant, commemorating his short life.

EDCS-13003351.translation: Gaius Iulius Urbanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003351.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Gaius Iulius Urbanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003352.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Iulius Urbanus lived for 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003352.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Iulius Urbanus, acknowledging the Roman gods of the underworld and his burial place, dating probably to the Imperial era.

EDCS-13003353.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Ettus (or Ettius) lived for 21 years.
EDCS-13003353.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Ettus (or Ettius), a young man who lived for 21 years, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-13003354.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Matrona lived for 65 years.
EDCS-13003354.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Matrona, who lived to the age of 65, and is addressed to the Roman underworld deities.

EDCS-13003355.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Mulsula lived for 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003355.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Mulsula, recording her age at death as 40 and her burial place, addressed to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-13003356.translation: Lucia Iulia Mustela, daughter of Lucius, lived for 13 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003356.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Lucia Iulia Mustela, who died at age 13, including a traditional wish for the deceased's peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003357.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Octavia lived for 31 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003357.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Octavia, who lived for 31 years, noting her burial place and invoking the Divine Manes.

EDCS-13003358.translation: To the Divine Manes. Istanus Aventius lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13003358.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Istanus Aventius, who died at the age of 20, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-13003360.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Licinius Celsus lived for 105 years.
EDCS-13003360.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Licinius Celsus, who achieved the remarkable age of 105, addressed to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-13003361.translation: Lucius Licinius Tertullus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 101 years. He is buried here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-13003361.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Tertullus, noting his remarkable age of 101, his lineage, tribe, and burial place, and includes a wish for peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003362.translation: Manilia, freedwoman of Lucius, of Amcismi kart, lies here. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003362.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Manilia, a freedwoman, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-13003364.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quintus Anlius Rusticus, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-13003365.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, with the name Marc[...] partially preserved.

EDCS-13003366.translation: Marcia, daughter of Optatus, lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003366.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcia, the daughter of Optatus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13003367.translation: To the memory of Maxsima, a most innocent girl. Restitutus(?) [erected this].
EDCS-13003367.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Restitutus(?) to the memory of Maxsima, described as a most innocent girl.

EDCS-13003368.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Minucius Felix lived 30 years.
EDCS-13003368.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Quintus Minucius Felix, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-13003369.translation: To the Divine Manes. Minucia Syntyche lived 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003369.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Minucia Syntyche, who lived 40 years and is interred at this site.

EDCS-13003371.translation: Mustiolus lived six years. He is here.
EDCS-13003371.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mustiolus, who lived for six years.

EDCS-13003372.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Nasidius Crispus, indicating he lived to the age of 95.

EDCS-13003375.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Oclatius Licinianus lived 6 years.
EDCS-13003375.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Lucius Oclatius Licinianus, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-13003376.translation: Oillia Matrona lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003376.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oillia Matrona, who lived 60 years, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-13003377.translation: To the Divine Manes. Itus Ovinius Saturus lived 66 years. His wife Minosa lived 51 years. They are buried here. May their bones rest well with you.
EDCS-13003377.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Itus Ovinius Saturus and his wife Minosa, recording their ages and burial together, with a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003378.translation: To the Divine Manes. Papiria Acuta lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003378.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Papiria Acuta, noting she lived for 60 years.

EDCS-13003379.translation: To the Divine Manes. Percennius Quintilianus lived for one month. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003379.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Percennius Quintilianus, who died as an infant and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13003380.translation: To the Divine Manes. PILLI(?) lived 15 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003380.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual named PILLI (uncertain reading) who lived 15 years, with a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003381.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pompeius Ingenuus lived 80 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003381.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius Ingenuus, noting he lived for 80 years and wishing for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003385.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publicius Custos lived 30 years.
EDCS-13003385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicius Custos, stating he lived for 30 years.

EDCS-13003387.translation: To the Divine Manes. Drupilius Neptunalis lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003387.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Drupilius Neptunalis, indicating he lived 25 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003391.translation: Scatia Fabula lived 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003391.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Scatia Fabula, recording her age of 40 and her burial place.

EDCS-13003393.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sittius Crescentianus lived 66 years.
EDCS-13003393.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittius Crescentianus, noting he lived for 66 years.

EDCS-13003394.translation: To the divine Spirits. Publius Sittius Gaetulus lived for three years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003394.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Gaetulus, likely erected by his family, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003396.translation: To the memory of Lucius Sittius Salvus, who lived for 3 years and 3 months. To a most loving son, he lies here.
EDCS-13003396.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Sittius Salvus by his parents, commemorating their young son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003398.translation: Marcus Sittius Victorianus made this for his wife.
EDCS-13003398.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marcus Sittius Victorianus created a monument for his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003399.translation: Sittia Candida lived for 75 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003399.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Candida, erected by her family or community, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003400.translation: To the divine Spirits. Sittia Marcella lived for 61 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003400.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Marcella, likely erected by her family, with a common valedictory formula, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003401.translation: To the memory of Sittia Novella, who lived for 38 years.
EDCS-13003401.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sittia Novella, likely erected by her family or friends, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003402.translation: Sittia Parthenope lived for 30 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003402.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Parthenope, presumably erected by her family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003403.translation: Spitia Tertia, daughter of Publius, lived for 90 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003403.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Spitia Tertia, daughter of Publius, recording her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003406.translation: To Tadia Rogata, who lived for 70 years. She lies here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003406.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tadia Rogata, likely by her family, including a common wish for peaceful rest, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003407.translation: To the divine Spirits. To Valeria Cheromena, who lived for 8 years and 6 days.
EDCS-13003407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Cheromena, a young child, likely erected by her parents, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003408.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valeria Maximina lived for 35 years.
EDCS-13003408.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Maximina, indicating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003409.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Veneria lived for [---] years.
EDCS-13003409.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a woman named Veneria, recording her existence and likely her age at death.

EDCS-13003410.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Vennius Marcianus lived for 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vennius Marcianus, stating his age at death and his burial place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003411.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Victor lived for 10 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-13003411.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Victor, noting his young age at death and including a common funerary wish, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003414.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a woman named Hilara, recording her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003415.translation: To Caecina Matrona. She lived for 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecina Matrona, stating her age at death and burial location, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003437.text_edition: Divo Commodo divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici filio fratri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici propagatori imperii pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori  consuli II
EDCS-13003437.translation: To the deified Commodus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the fifth time, Imperator, Consul for the second time.
EDCS-13003437.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Emperor Commodus, highlighting his lineage and imperial titles, likely erected by his brother Septimius Severus in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003438.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Octavia Fellicula sic vixit annos LXXXXV hic sita est
EDCS-13003438.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Octavia Fellicula thus lived 95 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003438.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia Fellicula, noting her advanced age at death and her burial place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003440.text_edition: Genio Celtianis Augusto sacrum Lucius Manilius Luci filius Quirina Potens quo numinis eius adiumentum senserit ex HS VIIIocto milibus sua pecunia fecit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-13003440.translation: Sacred to the Divine Genius of the Celtiani. Lucius Manilius Potens, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, because he experienced the help of its divinity, made this with his own money of eight thousand sesterces, and he also dedicated it.
EDCS-13003440.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the Genius of the Celtiani by Lucius Manilius Potens, who funded and dedicated it himself in gratitude for divine aid, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003441.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto Armeniaco imperatori II tribunicia potestate III consuli II proconsuli decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-13003441.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus Armeniacus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, imperator for the second time, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, proconsul, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-13003441.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Lucius Verus, listing his imperial titles and lineage, funded by public money through a decree of the decurions, dating to his co-reign with Marcus Aurelius (circa 163-166 CE).

EDCS-13003444.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum coniugi Imperatoris Caesaris divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici fili divi Commodi fratris divi Antonini Pii nepotis divi Hadriani pronepotis divi Traiani Parthici abnepotis divi Nervae adnepotis Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XIII imperatoris XI consulis III proconsulis propagatoris imperii fortissimi felicissimique principis Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi fili divi Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepotis divi Antonini Pii pronepotis divi Hadriani abnepotis divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepotis Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII consulis II proconsulis fortissimi felicissimique principis patris patriae nobilissimi et super omnes principes indulgentissimo sic res publica Castelli Celtianis decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-13003444.translation: To Julia Augusta, Mother of the Camps, wife of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the thirteenth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the third time, proconsul, propagator of the empire, the bravest and most fortunate prince; son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva; and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the second time, proconsul, bravest and most fortunate prince, father of the country, most noble and most indulgent above all princes, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva. Thus the Republic of Castelli Celtianenses, by decree of the decurions, with public money [erected this].
EDCS-13003444.summary: This lengthy inscription records the dedication of a monument, likely a statue or architectural element, by the public funds and decree of the decurions of Castelli Celtianenses, in honor of Julia Augusta, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, during the co-rule of Septimius Severus and Caracalla (c. 205 CE).

EDCS-13003445.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Oppius Cai filius Quirina Rogatus veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis vixit annos LV hic situs est
EDCS-13003445.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Oppius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Rogatus, a veteran of the First Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Oppius Rogatus, a veteran of Legio I Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, erected by unknown individuals.

EDCS-13003446.text_edition: Quintus Bottius Titi filius Quirina Senecio magister pagi aediliciae iuris dictionis suo? nomine? etet Crescentis filiorum suorum statuis columnis
EDCS-13003446.translation: Quintus Bottius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, Senecio, master of the vicus with aedilician jurisdiction, in his own name and that of his sons, Crescentis, [erected these] statues and columns.
EDCS-13003446.summary: This inscription records Quintus Bottius Senecio, a local magistrate, dedicating statues and columns on behalf of himself and his son Crescentis.

EDCS-13003447.text_edition: Bottio  filio Quirina Senecioni filio ob munificentiam Luci Botti Senecionis patris eius Celtianenses aere conlato posuerunt decreto decurionum
EDCS-13003447.translation: To Bottius Senecio, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, in recognition of the generosity of his father Lucius Bottius Senecio, the Celtianenses placed this with money collected by contribution, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13003447.summary: This inscription records the Celtianenses dedicating a monument to Bottius Senecio, son of Quintus, acknowledging the generosity of his father, Lucius Bottius Senecio, funded by public contribution and decree of the decurions.

EDCS-13003449.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aemilius Aculus vixit annis XXXXV hic situs est
EDCS-13003449.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aemilius Aculus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003449.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aemilius Aculus, noting his age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-13003450.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aemilius Eroticus vixit annos LXI hic situs est
EDCS-13003450.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Aemilius Eroticus lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aemilius Eroticus, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13003451.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilius Faustus vixit annos XL hic situs
EDCS-13003451.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilius Faustus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003451.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Faustus, stating his age and place of burial.

EDCS-13003452.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Aemilius Luci filius Quirina Fortunatus vixit annos LXXXI hic situs est
EDCS-13003452.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Fortunatus, lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003452.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Fortunatus, a man from the Quirina tribe, recording his advanced age and burial.

EDCS-13003453.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Aemilius Cai filius Quirina Martialis vixit annos XXXX hic situs est
EDCS-13003453.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Aemilius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Martialis, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003453.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Aemilius Martialis, of the Quirina tribe, indicating his age and burial site.

EDCS-13003454.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aemilius Musicus vixit annos LXXX hic situs est
EDCS-13003454.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Aemilius Musicus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003454.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aemilius Musicus, stating his age and place of burial.

EDCS-13003455.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aemil=Tia#Aemilia#AEMTIA Marsa vixit annos LX
EDCS-13003455.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aemilia Marsa lived for 60 years.
EDCS-13003455.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Aemilia Marsa, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-13003456.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Annaenius Felicio vixit annos LXXX hic situs est
EDCS-13003456.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Annaenius Felicio lived for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003456.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Annaenius Felicio, recording his lifespan of 80 years and his burial place.

EDCS-13003457.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Annius.
EDCS-13003457.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Annius, likely marking his tomb.

EDCS-13003458.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Antistia  filia Quirina Catulla vixit annos LXV hic sita est
EDCS-13003458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Antistia Quirina Catulla, daughter, lived for 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003458.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Antistia Quirina Catulla, the daughter, who lived to be 65 and is interred at this spot.

EDCS-13003459.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Cai filia Ceriola vixit annos XXXX hic sita est
EDCS-13003459.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Antonia Ceriola, daughter of Gaius, lived for 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003459.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Ceriola, daughter of Gaius, noting her lifespan of 40 years and her burial.

EDCS-13003460.text_edition: Antonia Publi filia Monna vixit annos XX
EDCS-13003460.translation: Antonia Monna, daughter of Publius, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13003460.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Monna, daughter of Publius, indicating she died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13003461.text_edition: Anntonia sic Nampame vixit annos XXXV hic sita est
EDCS-13003461.translation: Antonia Nampame lived for 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003461.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Nampame, recording her death at age 35 and her place of burial.

EDCS-13003462.text_edition: Arruntia Burucbal Quinti Arrunti filia vixit annos LXXXV hic sita est
EDCS-13003462.translation: Arruntia Burucbal, daughter of Quintus Arruntius, lived for 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003462.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arruntia Burucbal, daughter of Quintus Arruntius, detailing her lifespan of 85 years and her interment.

EDCS-13003463.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Axilia Optata vixit annos CX
EDCS-13003463.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Axilia Optata lived for 110 years.
EDCS-13003463.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Axilia Optata, who lived for an impressive 110 years.

EDCS-13003464.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Bennius Fusus vixit annos LXXV hic situs est
EDCS-13003464.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Bennius Fusus lived for 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003464.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Bennius Fusus, stating he lived for 75 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-13003465.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Bennius Iulianus vixit annos XXXX hic situs est
EDCS-13003465.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Bennius Iulianus lived for 40 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003465.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Gaius Bennius Iulianus, who lived for 40 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003466.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Bennius Luci filius Saturninus vixit annos LXI hic situs est
EDCS-13003466.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Bennius Saturninus, son of Lucius, lived for 61 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003466.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Bennius Saturninus, son of Lucius, who lived for 61 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003467.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius B Lappius vixit annos L hic situs est
EDCS-13003467.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius B. Lappius lived for 50 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003467.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius B. Lappius, who lived for 50 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003468.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Bombius Marci filius Quirina Cicero filius vixit annos XXV hic situs est
EDCS-13003468.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Bombius Cicero, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003468.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Marcus Bombius Cicero, son of Marcus and a member of the Quirina tribe, who lived for 25 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003469.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Bombius Sexti filius Quirina Messor vixit annos LXXV hic situs est
EDCS-13003469.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Bombius Messor, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 75 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003469.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Gaius Bombius Messor, son of Sextus and a member of the Quirina tribe, who lived for 75 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003545.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Quintus Pomponius Fortunatus, son of Quintus and member of the Quirina tribe, noting his age and burial site.

EDCS-13003546.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Caius Propertius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13003547.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus C Honoratus vixit annos XXXX
EDCS-13003547.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus C. Honoratus lived 40 years.
EDCS-13003547.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Marcus C. Honoratus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-13003548.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Secundus vixit annos XVII
EDCS-13003548.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Secundus lived 17 years.

EDCS-13003551.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Honorata, identifying her parents and her age at death.

EDCS-13003552.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sittia Quetosa vixit annos XXXV hic sita est

EDCS-13003553.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Stercula, noting her age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-13003554.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Trebellia Marcellina, daughter of Gaius, indicating her age at death and burial place.

EDCS-13003555.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Valerius Iulianus vixit annos  hic situs est
EDCS-13003555.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Julianus lived for [---] years. He lies here.

EDCS-13003639.text_edition: MarcellIa#Marcella#MARCELIA vixit annos LXXXXV hic sita est ossa tibi bene quiescant
EDCS-13003639.translation: Marcella lived 95 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcella, recording her age at death and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003641.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, with only parts of names and a number, possibly an age, visible.

EDCS-13003643.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named QSIIESII, with their age at death incomplete.

EDCS-13200067.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Libela, a stage actor of the streets, lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200067.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Libela, a stage actor, who lived 50 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-13200068.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Murinus, a pious stage actor, lived 52 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Murinus, a pious stage actor, indicating he lived 52 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13200069.translation: Panaria, companion from Astigi. She is buried here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-13200069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Panaria, identified as a companion from Astigi, with a traditional wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13200073.translation: Valerius Dativus, the father, made this sacred to the Spirits of the Departed for his most exceptional daughter, Ururia, who lived 25 years and six months. The sand-sartor, master, placed this for his sweetest daughter. She is buried here. GVN
EDCS-13200073.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Dativus to the spirits of the departed for his exceptionally sweet daughter, Ururia, who died at 25 years and six months, likely erected by her father, a sand-sartor and master.

EDCS-13200074.translation: Quintus A... F... lived 40 years. He rests here.

EDCS-13200076.translation: Lucius Accius Mascel, he lived 50 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200077.translation: Birenius Adiutor, he lived 50 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Admis, she lived 20 years. She is buried here.

EDCS-13200080.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aebutius Crescentianus, he lived 60 years.

EDCS-13200081.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aebutius Maurus, he lived 75 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200082.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aebutius Severus, he lived 35 years. He is here, may he rest well.

EDCS-13200083.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aebutia Maura, she lived 25 years.

EDCS-13200085.translation: Lucius Aelius Lucanus, he lived 75 years.

EDCS-13200086.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Marcella, she lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13200086.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, marking the burial of Aelia Marcella, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-13200087.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Prisca lived 85 years. May your bones rest well. Victoricus, her son, made this for his most uncommon mother.

EDCS-13200089.translation: To the memory of Sextus Aemilius Felicianus, a lover of his own blood and a benefactor of his family. Aemilia Gargilia made this for her most loving brother. He lived 65 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-13200093.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Aemilius Sodales lived 27 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-13200094.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aemilia Atiali, likely a funerary monument given the context of the surrounding inscriptions.

EDCS-13200095.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aemilia Grata lived 30 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-13200096.translation: To Aemilia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, wife, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-13200118.translation: Sextus Arridius Felix lived 85 years; he lies here. Iulia Rogata lived 51 years; she lies here, his wife.

EDCS-13200120.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arria Luculla lived 5 years.

EDCS-13200121.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arria Quinita lived 35 years. May your bones rest well.

EDCS-13200122.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arria Successa lived 80 years; she lies here.

EDCS-13200128.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Attius Urbanus lived 55 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200129.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Attia Matronica lived 92 years.

EDCS-13200130.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Auficia Matanica lived 35 years.

EDCS-13200131.translation: Publius Aufidius Cotta, son of Publius, of the Aemilian voting tribe, is buried here. He lived 45 years.

EDCS-13200135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Balinor[is] Cirtensis, a most sweet son, he lived five years.
EDCS-13200135.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits, commemorating Balinoris Cirtensis, a beloved son who died at the age of five.

EDCS-13200136.translation: Publius Basilius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Quirina voting tribe, lived 90 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200137.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Basilius Meleager lived 55 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13200138.translation: Basilia Felicula lived 80 years. She is buried here.

EDCS-13200141.translation: To the Divine Shades. Buria Ianuaria lived 7 years; she lies here.

EDCS-13200144.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Caecilius Faustus lived 91 years; he lies here.

EDCS-13200146.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Publius Caecilius Gaeticulus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13200165.translation: Calolia Procula lived 40 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calolia Procula, stating her age at death and burial place.

EDCS-13200166.translation: To the Divine Manes. In memory of Calpurnia Maxima, she lived 60 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200166.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Calpurnia Maxima, recording her age and burial spot.

EDCS-13200167.translation: Calviatus Cerutus lived 70 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200167.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calviatus Cerutus, noting his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-13200168.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Caninius Modestus, son of Publius, lived 11 years, 4 months, 13 days; he lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-13200168.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Caninius Modestus by his family, specifying his exact age at death and including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-13200169.translation: Caninia Marcella, the sweetest daughter, who lived [one] year, 4 months, 21 days; she lies here.
EDCS-13200169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caninia Marcella, a beloved daughter, detailing her very young age at death.

EDCS-13200170.translation: In memory of Caninia Octavena, a most rare woman. Her father, grieving at her fate, placed this. She lived 27 years, 3 months; she lies here.
EDCS-13200170.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a grieving father in memory of his exceptional daughter, Caninia Octavena, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13200171.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Cantinius Saterus lived 80 years.
EDCS-13200171.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Lucius Cantinius Saterus, who lived to an old age of eighty years.

EDCS-13200172.translation: Publius Carbonius(?) Crescens, he lived 70 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13200172.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Publius Carbonius(?) Crescens, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-13200173.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Carisius Conductor lived 12 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-13200173.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Carisius Conductor, who died at the age of 12, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-13200174.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Cassius Martialis, noting his name and a partially preserved age.

EDCS-13200175.translation: To the memory of the Scantiana gens. Satur and Cassia Queta, his wife, Satur the father, made this for all of his own and all his most innocent family members. Read and do not be surprised: this road has such dust.
EDCS-13200175.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Satur to the memory of the Scantiana family, including his wife Cassia Queta, and his other innocent family members, featuring a peculiar closing remark about the road's dust.

EDCS-13200176.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Castricata Hospita lived 62 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200176.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Castricata Hospita, who lived 62 years, with a valediction for her bones.

EDCS-13200177.translation: Cata lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13200177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cata, stating she lived 60 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13200178.translation: [---]ius Catulus, he is buried here, he lived 85 years.
EDCS-13200178.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Catulus, whose full first name is missing, stating he lived 85 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13200179.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Tiberius Claudius Candidus lived 51 years.
EDCS-13200179.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tiberius Claudius Candidus, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-13200180.translation: Gaius Claudius Caudinus lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200180.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Claudius Caudinus, indicating he lived 35 years and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-13200181.translation: Gaius Claudius Honoratus lived approximately 30 years.
EDCS-13200181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Claudius Honoratus, stating he lived approximately 30 years.

EDCS-13200182.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Claudius Martialis, pious, lived for 2 years.
EDCS-13200182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Claudius Martialis, who died at the age of two, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-13200183.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudius Mudius Virilio lived for 65 years.
EDCS-13200183.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Claudius Mudius Virilio, who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-13200185.translation: Clodia Selene, daughter of Marcus, lived for 23 years. She is laid here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-13200185.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Selene, daughter of Marcus, who died at 23 years of age, buried with a common valedictory wish.

EDCS-13200187.translation: To the Divine Manes. Clodius Ermetio lived for 51 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-13200187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Ermetio, who lived to the age of 51 and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13200188.translation: Clodius Fidus lived for 25 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-13200188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Fidus, who died at the age of 25 and is interred here.

EDCS-13200189.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Clodius Inennus lived for 3 years and 5 months. He is laid here.
EDCS-13200189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Clodius Inennus, who died at a very young age of 3 years and 5 months.

EDCS-13200190.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Clodius Paltanus lived for 30 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-13200190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Clodius Paltanus, who died at the age of 30 and is interred at this spot.

EDCS-13200191.translation: Gaius Clodius Primus lived for 70 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-13200191.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Clodius Primus, who died at the age of 70 and is buried here.

EDCS-13200192.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Clodius Saturus, the sweetest son, who lived for 8 years.
EDCS-13200192.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Clodius Saturus by his family, likely his parents, remembering their "sweetest son" who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-13200193.translation: Clodia Novella, daughter of Lucius, lived for 25 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-13200193.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Novella, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 25 and is interred at this location.

EDCS-13200194.translation: To the Divine Manes. Clodia Satura, who lived 35 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Satura, who died at the age of 35 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200196.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Co. Hospita, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-13200196.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Co. Hospita, who lived to be 70 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200197.translation: To the Divine Manes. Co. Betius Senior, who lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200197.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Co. Betius Senior, who died at the age of 20 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200198.translation: To the Divine Manes. Co. Cas., who lived 31 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Co. Cas., who died at the age of 31 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200199.translation: To the Divine Manes. Coelius Aper, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-13200199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Coelius Aper, who lived to be 60 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200200.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Coelius Colonus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Coelius Colonus, son of Quintus, a member of the Quirina tribe, who died at the age of 20 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200201.translation: Sextus Coelius Secundus, who lived 9 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200201.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Coelius Secundus, who died at the age of 9 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200202.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cominia Getula, who lived 60 years, is here. V. O.
EDCS-13200202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cominia Getula, who lived to be 60 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200203.translation: To the Divine Manes. Heris, son of Gaius Considius Marcianus, who lived 9 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13200203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heris, son of Gaius Considius Marcianus, who died at the age of 9 and was buried at this site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13200204.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Lucius Cornelius, son of Gaius, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-13200205.translation: Cornelius Asclepides lived for 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200205.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Cornelius Asclepides.

EDCS-13200206.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelius Concessus lived for 91 years.
EDCS-13200206.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Cornelius Concessus and his age at death.

EDCS-13200207.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus lived for 90 years.
EDCS-13200207.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, honoring Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus and his age.

EDCS-13200208.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Cornelius Matelio lived for 45 years.
EDCS-13200208.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, recording the name and age of Cornelius Matelio.

EDCS-13200209.translation: Lucius Cornelius Nampulus lived for [?]X[?] years. He is buried here. Lucius Cornelius Saturninus lived for 5[?] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200209.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the names, ages, and burial places of Lucius Cornelius Nampulus and Lucius Cornelius Saturninus, likely on different panels or sides of the same stone.

EDCS-13200210.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelius Optatus lived for 41 years.
EDCS-13200210.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Cornelius Optatus and his age at death.

EDCS-13200211.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelius Primitivus lived for 33 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200211.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, marking the burial place and age of Cornelius Primitivus.

EDCS-13200212.translation: Cornelius Rufus, L. Corn[---], lived for 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200212.summary: This is a funerary inscription, partially preserved, recording Cornelius Rufus and another individual, noting their age and burial place.

EDCS-13200213.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sextus Cornelius Satu[---]. Cornelia Maxim[---].
EDCS-13200213.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Sextus Cornelius and Cornelia Maxim[---], likely two individuals from the same family.

EDCS-13200214.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Callityche lived for 33 years. She is buried here. P. P. C., her husband.
EDCS-13200214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Cornelia Callityche, her age, and her burial place, set up by her husband.

EDCS-13200215.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Marcia lived 40 years.
EDCS-13200215.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Marcia, stating her name and age at death.

EDCS-13200216.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription commemorating Crepereia Talusa and her exceptionally long life.

EDCS-13200218.translation: Crispus lived 60 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13200218.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Crispus, including his name and age at death.

EDCS-13200219.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Cuculnius Fortunatus lived 80 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200219.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Cuculnius Fortunatus, providing his name, age, and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13200220.translation: C. Vnonia Hermana, daughter of Lucius, lived 7 years.
EDCS-13200220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named C. Vnonia Hermana, noting her parentage and age at death.

EDCS-13200221.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cupius Dexter lived 85 years. To the Divine Manes. Iulia Monula lived 52 years.
EDCS-13200221.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely from a shared monument, for Cupius Dexter and Iulia Monula, detailing their names and ages.

EDCS-13200222.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Curculio lived 20 years.
