EDCS-20800611.translation: To Lucius Novius Crispinus Martialis Saturninus, consul designate, imperial legate with propraetorian power of the province of Africa, proconsul of Gallia Narbonensis, imperial legate of the First Italic Legion, imperial legate with judicial power for Asturia and Gallaecia, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor with propraetorian power for the province of Macedonia, military tribune of the Ninth Hispanic Legion, commissioner for roads, sevir of Roman equites. Veterans of the Third Augustan Legion who began their military service when Glabrio and Torquatus, likewise Asiaticus II and Aquilinus, were consuls.
EDCS-20800611.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a prominent Roman senator, Lucius Novius Crispinus Martialis Saturninus, detailing his extensive senatorial career (cursus honorum), erected by veterans of the Third Augustan Legion, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD based on the consuls mentioned.

EDCS-20800612.text_edition: Aeliae Prosperae clarissimi feminae coniugi Cai Pomponi Magni legati Auggustorum pro praetore clarissimi viri praesidis stratores
EDCS-20800612.translation: To Aelia Prospera, most distinguished lady, wife of Gaius Pomponius Magnus, imperial legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, most distinguished man, governor. His grooms.
EDCS-20800612.summary: This inscription is a dedication by grooms (stratores) to Aelia Prospera, the wife of Gaius Pomponius Magnus, a distinguished imperial legate and governor, likely erected in a public or semi-public context.

EDCS-20800613.text_edition: Marco Valerio Maximiano legato Augusti pro praetore legionis III Augustae consuli praesidi rarissimo Refrius Maximus |(centurio) legionis III Augustae strator eius
EDCS-20800613.translation: To Marcus Valerius Maximianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power of the Third Augustan Legion, consul, most exceptional governor. Refrius Maximus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, his groom.
EDCS-20800613.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Refrius Maximus, a centurion and groom, to Marcus Valerius Maximianus, a distinguished senatorial legate of the Third Augustan Legion who also held consular rank and governorship, likely erected during the latter's service or shortly after.

EDCS-20800617.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Maximo legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro praesidi benignissimo Aurelii Crescens et Gainas ||(centuriones) legionis III Augustae Severianae
EDCS-20800617.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Maximus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, most distinguished man, most benevolent governor. Aurelius Crescens and Gainas, centurions of the Third Augustan Legion Severiana.
EDCS-20800617.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two centurions of the Third Augustan Legion Severiana, Aurelius Crescens and Gainas, to Marcus Ulpius Maximus, a distinguished imperial legate and governor, likely erected to honor his benevolent rule.

EDCS-20800619.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Publius Aelius Crescentianus, son of Publius, notary to the legate in the office of Juvenalis, Praetorian Prefect, who died having lived twenty-two years and served four years. Aelia Processa, his mother, made this for her innocent son.
EDCS-20800619.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Processa to her son, Publius Aelius Crescentianus, a notary who served in the office of a Praetorian Prefect, detailing his age and length of service.

EDCS-20800627.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, to the eternal memory of Claudius Maximus Sabinus, primus pilus of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 76 years. Marcia Aurelia, his wife, and the freedmen Maximi, Hermes, Victor, and Hermias, made this for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-20800627.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Claudius Maximus Sabinus, a primus pilus of the Third Augustan Legion, by his wife Marcia Aurelia and his freedmen, Hermes, Victor, and Hermias, commemorating his long life and service.

EDCS-20800628.translation: To Titus Flavius Maximus, prefect of the Third Augustan Legion. The heirs of Iulius Secundus, formerly a centurion of the aforementioned legion, to whom the same Maximus had bequeathed in his will the task of building a monument for himself with 12,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800628.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a testamentary instruction, where the heirs of a centurion, Iulius Secundus, erected a monument for Titus Flavius Maximus, prefect of the Third Augustan Legion, as stipulated in Maximus's will.

EDCS-20800630.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Publius Furius Rusticus, prefect of the First Cohort of Asturians in the province of Lower Britannia, tribune of soldiers of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, who lived 40 years. Publius Furius Rusticus, his father, made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-20800630.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Furius Rusticus senior for his son, also Publius Furius Rusticus, who served as a prefect of a cohort in Britannia and a military tribune in the Third Augustan Legion, commemorating his son's short life and military career.

EDCS-20800636.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Flavia Iuliosa, my wife, who lived 27 years. Marcus Servilius Fortunatus, a veteran, who brought back his wife's remains across seas and lands from the province of Dacia.
EDCS-20800636.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Servilius Fortunatus, a veteran, to his wife Flavia Iuliosa, commemorating her age and his arduous journey to repatriate her remains from the province of Dacia.

EDCS-20800640.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Sextus Verteblasius Victor, first duumvir of the municipality of Lambaesis, who lived 80 years. Sextus Verteblasius Victor, prefect of cavalry, his son, made this for his most exceptional father.
EDCS-20800640.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sextus Verteblasius Victor, a prefect of cavalry, to his father, also Sextus Verteblasius Victor, a former leading magistrate (duumvir) of the municipality of Lambaesis, commemorating his father's long life and civic office.

EDCS-20800661.text_edition: legato Augusti provinciae Numidiae et legionis III Augustae Gallienae Aemilius Florus beneficiarius domicurius eius patrono praestantissimo
EDCS-20800661.translation: To the legate of Augustus, of the province of Numidia and of the Third Augustan Legion, Galliena. Aemilius Florus, his beneficiarius domicurius, dedicated this to his most excellent patron.
EDCS-20800661.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aemilius Florus, a beneficiarius, to his patron, a legate of Augustus in Numidia and commander of the Legio III Augusta Galliena.

EDCS-20800672.translation: Gaius Antonius Maternus, from Clunia, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, is buried here.
EDCS-20800672.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Antonius Maternus, a centurion of the Legio III Augusta, indicating his place of origin and burial.

EDCS-20800677.translation: Manius Aquilius Proculus, son of Manius, of the Aniensis tribe, from Ariminum, a commentariensis, lived 37 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-20800677.summary: This is a tombstone for Manius Aquilius Proculus, a commentariensis from Ariminum, recording his age and place of burial.

EDCS-20800681.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Secunda lived 35 years. Aufidius Felix, a duplicarius of the legion, made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-20800681.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aufidius Felix, a duplicarius of a legion, to his beloved wife, Aelia Secunda.

EDCS-20800688.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Hermias, a beneficiarius of the legion, lived 44 years. Flavia Nice, his wife and heir, made and dedicated this for her incomparable husband, using 1500 sesterces.
EDCS-20800688.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Flavia Nice to her incomparable husband, Aurelius Hermias, a beneficiarius of a legion, with the cost of the monument specified.

EDCS-20800691.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Aurelius Vibius Auinci, son of Titus, of the Aelia tribe, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, lived 60 years. Aurelia Ca[---] made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20800691.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aurelia Ca[---] to her well-deserving husband, Titus Aurelius Vibius Auinci, a centurion of the Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex.

EDCS-20800700.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Ulpia Mar[---], who lived [---] years. Marcus Calvisius Felix, a beneficiarius, made this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-20800700.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Marcus Calvisius Felix, a beneficiarius, to his most devoted mother, Ulpia Mar[---].

EDCS-20800701.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Marcus Calvisius Felix, a veteran, who lived 57 years. Marcus Calvisius Felix, a beneficiarius, made this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-20800701.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Marcus Calvisius Felix, a beneficiarius, to his most devoted father, also named Marcus Calvisius Felix, a veteran.

EDCS-20800705.translation: [---] Claudius Alexander, son of Tiberius, of the Collina tribe, [---] a veteran, formerly a custos armorum of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 56 years. [---] for himself this monument [---].
EDCS-20800705.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone for Claudius Alexander, a veteran who served as a custos armorum in the Legio III Augusta, indicating he likely prepared it for himself.

EDCS-20800733.translation: To Ulpia Prisca, an honorable woman, his dearest wife. Marcus Flavius Caecilius Telesphorianus, a frumentarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, [made this].
EDCS-20800733.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Flavius Caecilius Telesphorianus, a frumentarius of the Legio V Macedonica, to his dearest wife, Ulpia Prisca.

EDCS-20800735.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Flavius Clemens, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, lived 28 years. He is interred here.
EDCS-20800735.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Flavius Clemens, a centurion of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, erected upon his burial following his death at age 28.

EDCS-20800737.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Herculanus, son of Titus, of the Ulpia tribe, from Rtiaria, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex Antoniniana, lived 40 years. Cocceia Digna made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20800737.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cocceia Digna to her husband, Titus Flavius Herculanus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex Antoniniana, who died at age 40.

EDCS-20800743.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Titus Flavius Virilis, centurion of Legio II Augusta, centurion of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, centurion of Legio VI Victrix, centurion of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, centurion of Legio III Augusta, centurion of Legio III Parthica Severiana, junior spearman. He lived 70 years, served 45 years. Lollia Bodicca, his wife, and Flavius Victor and Victorinus, his sons, as heirs, took care of having this made from 1200 sesterces.
EDCS-20800743.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Titus Flavius Virilis, a career centurion who served in multiple legions, recorded by his wife and sons using funds from his estate.

EDCS-20800744.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Furius Candidus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, from Theveste, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion Antoniniana, lived 55 years. Iulia Artimisia, his wife, and Furius Candidus and Artimisia, his children and heirs, made and dedicated this for their dearest husband and father, from 2000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800744.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Artimisia, his wife, and their children, Furius Candidus and Artimisia, to Marcus Furius Candidus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta Antoniniana, who lived to be 55 years old.

EDCS-20800753.translation: To Gaius Iulius Fidus, a cavalryman of the Seventh Lusitanian Cohort, who lived 25 years. Through Iulius Castus, his brother.
EDCS-20800753.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Fidus, a cavalryman from the Cohors VII Lusitanorum, who died at 25 years old, set up by his brother, Iulius Castus.

EDCS-20800757.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Bassus Sulpicianus, centurion of Legio II Traiana Fortis, also centurion of Legio XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis, also centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, also centurion of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, also centurion of Legio III Parthica Severiana. He lived 54 years, served 37 years. Iulia Saturnina, his wife, made this for her sweetest, well-deserving husband, with Iulius Bassus Donatus, procurator, taking care of it.
EDCS-20800757.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Saturnina to her husband, Iulius Bassus Sulpicianus, a career centurion who served in several legions and died at age 54 after 37 years of service, with Iulius Bassus Donatus supervising the work.

EDCS-20800760.translation: Iulius Donatus, a cavalryman of the Third Augustan Legion, from the optio ranks, lived 55 years and served 25 years. Made for a son and a deserving spouse.
EDCS-20800760.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Donatus, a cavalryman of Legio III Augusta who rose from the rank of optio, who lived for 55 years and served for 25 years, dedicated by his family.

EDCS-20800765.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Flavia Victoria, a most uncommon wife, who lived 33 years. Iulius Extricatus, an immunis of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, made this for his parents, as a pious son, according to their precept.
EDCS-20800765.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Flavia Victoria, who died at 33, by her husband Iulius Extricatus, an 'immunis' of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, who also honored his parents.

EDCS-20800766.translation: Vibia Optatina lived 41 years. Iulius Felix, a librarius, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-20800766.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Felix, a librarius, to his wife, Vibia Optatina, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-20900306.translation: In memory of Gaius Iulius Nepos, from Ammedara, a singularis infantryman. He lived 18 years. Gaius Iulius Verus, a consular beneficiarius, made this for his most dutiful brother.
EDCS-20900306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Nepos, an 18-year-old 'singularis' infantryman from Ammedara, dedicated by his brother, Gaius Iulius Verus, a 'beneficiarius consularis'.

EDCS-20900316.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Rogatus, a duplicarius of the Third Augustan Legion, Pious and Vindicatrix, lived for 47 years. Iulius Felix and Tertullus, his sons and heirs, made and dedicated this gift to their most innocent father.
EDCS-20900316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Rogatus, a duplicarius in the Legio III Augusta, erected by his sons and heirs, Iulius Felix and Tertullus.

EDCS-20900326.translation: Gaius Laetorius Pacatus, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 50 years. Crescentianus and Har[...]
EDCS-20900326.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Gaius Laetorius Pacatus, a standard-bearer of the Legio III Augusta, mentioning the names of those who set it up.

EDCS-20900344.translation: Gaius Mustius, son of Gaius, of the Aurelia tribe, Fortunatus from Lambaesis, a veteran formerly a standard-bearer.
EDCS-20900344.summary: This inscription records Gaius Mustius, a veteran from Lambaesis who previously served as a standard-bearer.

EDCS-20900347.translation: Aulus Nonius Longinus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his daughter, who was snatched away from him by a bitter death.
EDCS-20900347.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aulus Nonius Longinus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta, in memory of his deceased daughter.

EDCS-20900348.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades and to the eternal memory of Nonia Manliana, a maiden taken away, who lived 12 years, 9 months, 19 days. Gaius Nonius Manlianus, centurion of the Third Italic Legion, her father, and Iulia Clementina, her mother, saw to the erection of this for their sweetest daughter, at a cost of 2,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20900348.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nonia Manliana, a young girl, set up by her parents, Gaius Nonius Manlianus, a centurion of Legio III Italica, and Iulia Clementina, dated by the age of the deceased.

EDCS-20900353.translation: Marcus Oppius Antiochianus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, made this in memory of Memmia Faustina.
EDCS-20900353.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Marcus Oppius Antiochianus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta, in memory of Memmia Faustina.

EDCS-20900370.translation: To the Divine Shades of Marcus Silius Faustus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Ammaedara, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who died in Parthia. He lived 41 years. Lucius Silius Rufinus, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, and Silius Quietus, a soldier of the same legion, made this for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-20900370.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Silius Faustus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta who died in Parthia, erected by his brothers, Lucius Silius Rufinus (a standard-bearer) and Silius Quietus (a soldier of the same legion).

EDCS-20900381.translation: Lucius Tonneius Martialis, a wax-worker of the legion, made this for himself and his family while he was alive.
EDCS-20900381.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Lucius Tonneius Martialis, a cerarius (wax-worker) of the legion, for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-21000668.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino Pio Felici Augusto domino nostro Valerio Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-21000668.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus; to our Lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate Augustus; to our Lord Valerius Licinianus Licinius, Pious, Fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-21000668.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-reigning Roman emperors Constantine I, Maximinus Daia, and Licinius, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-21100001.translation: To Valerius Priscianus, who lived 5 years, 10 months, 6 days. Valerius Priscipinianus, speculator, his father, made this for his sweetest son. May the earth be light for you, Dalmatian.
EDCS-21100001.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young Valerius Priscianus, erected by his father, Valerius Priscipinianus, a speculator (scout or messenger), and includes a common funerary wish.

EDCS-21100003.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Vibius Felix, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, formerly a standard-bearer, made this for himself, while living, on his own property.
EDCS-21100003.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran standard-bearer of the Legio III Augusta, indicating he prepared his own tomb during his lifetime.

EDCS-21100005.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Superatus, dearest freedman and foster-child, who lived 9 years. Marcus Ulpius Optatus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, made this.
EDCS-21100005.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a centurion of Legio III Augusta for his young freedman and foster-child, Marcus Ulpius Superatus.

EDCS-21100007.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades and to eternal memory. Furia Emerita, wife and heir, made this for Lucius Volumnius Crescens, an 'optio' of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 30 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-21100007.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Furia Emerita to her husband, Lucius Volumnius Crescens, an optio (junior officer) of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100052.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Publius Aulius Apolinarius, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, transferred from the Third Gallic Legion, who lived 47 years and served 25 years. Julia Lucilla, his sweet mother [made this].
EDCS-21100052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Lucilla to her son, Publius Aulius Apolinarius, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, transferred from Legio III Gallica.

EDCS-21100069.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Rufinus, veteran of the Third Adiutrix Legion. Grania Silvana, his wife, placed this for her husband.
EDCS-21100069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Grania Silvana to her husband, Gaius Caecilius Rufinus, a veteran of Legio III Adiutrix.

EDCS-21100092.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maturia Rogata lived 70 years. Lucius Cornelius Flaccus, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, made this for his most loving mother.
EDCS-21100092.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Cornelius Flaccus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, for his mother, Maturia Rogata.

EDCS-21100114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Flavius Sigi(?), soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, transferred from the Third Gallic Legion, lived 40 years.
EDCS-21100114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Flavius Sigi(?), a soldier of Legio III Augusta who had been transferred from Legio III Gallica.

EDCS-21100156.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. May the earth be light for you, Palladius. Gaius Iulius Nestor Junior lived 7 years and 9 months. Gaius Iulius Nestor, veteran father, transferred from the Third Gallic Legion to the Third Augustan Legion, and Agria Fabiana, mother, made this for their sweetest son.
EDCS-21100156.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Nestor, a veteran of Legio III Augusta (originally from Legio III Gallica), and his wife Agria Fabiana, for their young son, Gaius Iulius Nestor Junior, and also likely for Palladius, possibly another child.

EDCS-21100163.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Saturninus, equestrian of the Third Augustan Antoniniana Legion, lived 40 years. Propertia Quinta, his wife and heir, placed this; he is buried here.
EDCS-21100163.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Propertia Quinta to her husband, Gaius Iulius Saturninus, an equestrian of Legio III Augusta Antoniniana.

EDCS-21100184.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades, to the memory of Publius Magnus Ianuarius, son of Publius, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion. Publius Magnius Abagalianus, his father, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-21100184.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Magnius Abagalianus to the memory of his son, Publius Magnus Ianuarius, a soldier of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100202.translation: To Horatia Saturnina, daughter of Quintus Horatius Fuscus, a veteran. Gaius Mustius Fortunatus, a veteran, made this for his most innocent wife.
EDCS-21100202.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by the veteran Gaius Mustius Fortunatus to his deceased wife, Horatia Saturnina, daughter of veteran Quintus Horatius Fuscus.

EDCS-21100207.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Octavius Barbarus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, lived for 25 years.
EDCS-21100207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Octavius Barbarus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-21100222.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Pontius Felix, a Caesarian soldier of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, who lived for 25 years. Julia Mapalica made this in her lifetime for herself and her son.
EDCS-21100222.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Pontius Felix, a soldier of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, at age 25, and was erected by his mother, Julia Mapalica, for herself and her son while she was still alive.

EDCS-21100228.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Perelia Ispenis, who lived for 46 years. Sallustius and Fortunatus, soldiers of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-21100228.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by the soldiers Sallustius and Fortunatus to their deserving mother, Perelia Ispenis.

EDCS-21100230.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Sallustius Datus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, lived for 50 years. Maximia Clara, his wife and heir, made this with her sons for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100230.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Maximia Clara, with her sons, for her deceased husband, Gaius Sallustius Datus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-21100240.translation: Gaius Stabilius Maternus of the Pomptina tribe, from Iuliobriga, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, from the century of Aprinus, 30 years old, with 13 years of service. Stabius Valerius Flavius Valerianus made this for his brother.
EDCS-21100240.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Stabius Valerius Flavius Valerianus for his brother, Gaius Stabilius Maternus, a soldier of Legio VII Gemina Felix from Iuliobriga, who died at age 30 after 13 years of service.

EDCS-21100245.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Pomponia Victoria lived for 80 years. Quintus Titinius Sallustianus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his most devoted grandmother.
EDCS-21100245.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Quintus Titinius Sallustianus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, to his most devoted grandmother, Pomponia Victoria, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-21100252.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Felix, a veteran, lived for 45 years. Julia made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100252.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Julia to her deserving husband, Valerius Felix, a veteran who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-21100269.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For [Lucius] Vibius Felix, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion Pia Vindex, who died fighting in a skirmish with the enemy. He lived for 21 years, 10 months, and 15 days, and served for 1 year, 1 month, and 2 days. Lucius Vibius Felix, his father, made this on his own property for his most beloved son.
EDCS-21100269.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Vibius Felix, a soldier of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, who died in battle against the enemy at the age of 21, and was erected by his father, Lucius Vibius Felix.

EDCS-21100276.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Quintus Volusius Rogatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-21100276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Volusius Rogatus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100284.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades and to good rest. For Aurelia Severa, who lived for 22 years, 11 months, 10 days. Heliodorus, freedman of the Augusti, from the archival office, made this for his most devoted and obedient wife.
EDCS-21100284.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Heliodorus, a freedman from the imperial archives, to his most devoted wife, Aurelia Severa, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-21100289.translation: --- for the honor of the aedileship --- by Arteor and Acilius, aediles ---
EDCS-21100289.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record a dedication or action related to the honor of the aedileship, possibly by aediles named Arteor and Acilius.

EDCS-21100327.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the name and age of Aelius Iamlicus.

EDCS-21100330.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilius Arisus lived 50 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-21100330.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by the sons of Aemilius Arisus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-21100338.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Aelia Benigna, who ---
EDCS-21100338.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aelia Benigna.

EDCS-21100339.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aelia Castula lived 50 years. His wife made this for her husband.
EDCS-21100339.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her husband, Aelia Castula, who lived 50 years, implying a possible error in 'Aelia' and that 'uxor' should refer to the deceased's wife and 'viro' to the deceased husband.

EDCS-21100340.translation: To the memory of Aelia Eunoea, a most uncommon wife. Publius Aelius Barytimus made this.
EDCS-21100340.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Publius Aelius Barytimus to the memory of his exceptional wife, Aelia Eunoea.

EDCS-21100341.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Fortunata lived 40 years. Nampamo made this for his wife.
EDCS-21100341.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Nampamo to his wife, Aelia Fortunata, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-21300144.translation: Bibenia Aventina lived 25 years. Pompeius Extricatus made this for his wife. May you rest well here.
EDCS-21300144.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Pompeius Extricatus to his wife, Bibenia Aventina, who died at the age of 25, including a farewell wish.

EDCS-21300149.translation: To Burrenia Fortunata, a most devoted wife. She lived for 22 years.
EDCS-21300149.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an unnamed individual to his most devoted wife, Burrenia Fortunata, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-21300208.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Clodia Maxima lived for 40 years. Arruntius Donatus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-21300208.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Arruntius Donatus to his wife, Clodia Maxima, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-21300216.translation: [...]ius, and also to Comicia Emerita, [and to] Comicius Emeritus, [to their] parents.
EDCS-21300216.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Comicia Emerita and Comicius Emeritus, presumably by their children.

EDCS-21600156.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum Lucius Cestius Successus filius et heres Luci Cesti Galli fideiussoris Flavi Natalis pollicitatoris huius statuae iussus ex decreto Fontei Frontiniani legati Augusti pro praetore clarissimi viri adiectis ad HS IIItria milia nummum quanti tunc hanc statuam idem Flavius Natalis rei publicae positurum se pollicitus erat HS IIIXXXXtria milia quadraginta nummum ex HS VIXXXXsex milibus quadraginta nummum posuit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-21600156.translation: Sacred to Victory Augusta. Lucius Cestius Successus, son and heir of Lucius Cestius Gallus, guarantor for Flavius Natalis, who promised this statue, by order of the decree of Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, a most distinguished man, added to the 3,000 sesterces, the amount for which Flavius Natalis had then promised to set up this statue for the public, an additional 3,040 sesterces, from a total of 6,040 sesterces, and erected and dedicated the same.
EDCS-21600156.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Victory Augusta by Lucius Cestius Successus, fulfilling a promise made by Flavius Natalis and guaranteed by his father, Lucius Cestius Gallus, with additional funds provided by himself and by decree of Fonteius Frontinianus, a senatorial legate.

EDCS-21600169.summary: This brief inscription simply records the names Diodorus Gemellus, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-21600171.translation: Domitius Crispinus lived for 42 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21600171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitius Crispinus, stating he lived for 42 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-21600177.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Domitius Secundus established his eternal home, where the remainder of his body might rest, during his lifetime.
EDCS-21600177.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Lucius Domitius Secundus prepared his own tomb during his lifetime.

EDCS-21600202.translation: To Fabricius Felix, Fabricius [---] his son and heir, made this.
EDCS-21600202.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Fabricius Felix, made by his son and heir, whose full name is partially lost.

EDCS-21600204.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Felicius lived for 5 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21600204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felicius, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-21600214.translation: Titus Flavius Datus, for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-21600214.summary: This is an inscription indicating that Titus Flavius Datus prepared a monument for himself while still alive.

EDCS-21600229.translation: To the memory of Flavia Iuliosa, his most devoted wife. Marcus Servilius Rogatus made this.
EDCS-21600229.summary: This funerary monument records the dedication by Marcus Servilius Rogatus to his deceased wife, Flavia Iuliosa.

EDCS-21600288.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To the memory and sanctity of Horatia Maxima, who lived 80 years. Quintus Horatius Pankalio, her freedman, made this for his patroness.
EDCS-21600288.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Quintus Horatius Pankalio, a freedman, to his deceased patroness, Horatia Maxima, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-21600311.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Felix lived 60 years. Victoria made this for her husband.
EDCS-21600311.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Victoria to her deceased husband, Gaius Iulius Felix, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-21600322.translation: Gaius Iulius Neon lived 8 years. Gaius Iulius Annianus lived 4 years. Iulia Ianuaria lived 5 years.
EDCS-21600322.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death for three children, Gaius Iulius Neon, Gaius Iulius Annianus, and Iulia Ianuaria.

EDCS-22000446.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Donata lived 35 years. Her husband made this for his well-deserving wife, for her eternal security.
EDCS-22000446.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, Iulia Donata, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-22000482.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Nonia Primitiva made this for the ashes of Iulia Pannonia, her most worthy patroness.
EDCS-22000482.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Nonia Primitiva to her deceased patroness, Iulia Pannonia.

EDCS-22000484.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To the memory of Iulia Prima, his mother. Aurelius Felix, her son.
EDCS-22000484.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Aurelius Felix to the memory of his deceased mother, Iulia Prima.

EDCS-22000549.translation: Luria Felica lived 16 years. Lurius Secundus, her brother.
EDCS-22000549.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Luria Felica at 16 years old, and the dedication by her brother, Lurius Secundus.

EDCS-22000555.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Magnius Vitalis lived 92 years. His devoted sons made this for his perpetual security.
EDCS-22000555.summary: This funerary monument records the dedication by the devoted sons of Publius Magnius Vitalis, who lived 92 years, ensuring his perpetual security.

EDCS-22000598.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Mocimus, son of Sumo, a Palmyrene, lived 30 years. His heir placed this.
EDCS-22000598.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by the heir of Mocimus, a Palmyrene, son of Sumo, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-22200701.translation: Gaius Pomponius Victor, son of Gaius, of the Papirian tribe, from Theveste, placed this for himself while alive and for his wife.
EDCS-22200701.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Pomponius Victor to himself and his wife, set up during his lifetime.

EDCS-22200732.translation: Publia Victoria lived for 35 years. Caecilius Felix (made this) for his wife.
EDCS-22200732.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication by Caecilius Felix to his wife, Publia Victoria, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-22700053.translation: Quintus Sutrius Rogatus lived for 50 years. Iulia Silvana made this from the ground up for her husband.
EDCS-22700053.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument built by Iulia Silvana for her husband, Quintus Sutrius Rogatus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-22700074.translation: Tullius Hermes, the father, made this for his daughter, Tullia Elaina, with the care of Iunius Pompeianus, her husband.
EDCS-22700074.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Tullius Hermes for his daughter, Tullia Elaina, with assistance from her husband, Iunius Pompeianus.

EDCS-22700099.text_edition: Dis Manibus Varenius Montanus vixit annis LXVII Iulia Licinia marito et sibi fecit
EDCS-22700099.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Varenius Montanus lived for 67 years. Iulia Licinia made this for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-22700099.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated to Varenius Montanus, who lived 67 years, and also to Iulia Licinia who erected it for herself and her husband.

EDCS-22700122.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vibullia Tertulla lived for 38 years and 1 month. Lucius Vibius Felix made this for his most rare and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-22700122.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Vibius Felix to his highly esteemed wife, Vibullia Tertulla, who lived 38 years and 1 month.

EDCS-22700129.translation: Lucius Vitruvius Verecundus, son of Sextus, of the Pollian tribe, a dutiful [---] lived for 7 years.
EDCS-22700129.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument for Lucius Vitruvius Verecundus, son of Sextus, who lived for seven years, presumably set up by his family.

EDCS-22700140.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. The remains of Lucius Umbrius Felix are placed here.
EDCS-22700140.summary: This inscription records the burial place of the remains of Lucius Umbrius Felix.

EDCS-23100031.text_edition: Genio patriae Augustae dedicante Iulio Lepido Tertullo legato Augusti pro praetore quod Lucius Pompeius Rogatus testamento suo ex HS XX (viginti milibus) nummum fieri iussit ordo Verecundensium faciendum curavit
EDCS-23100031.translation: To the Genius of the Augustan homeland, dedicated by Iulius Lepidus Tertullus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, which Lucius Pompeius Rogatus ordered to be made from 20,000 sesterces in his will; the order of the Verecundenses saw to its execution.
EDCS-23100031.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the Genius of the Augustan homeland, funded by Lucius Pompeius Rogatus' will and executed by the local council, with Iulius Lepidus Tertullus overseeing the dedication, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-23100038.text_edition: Numini domus Augustae et Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti Tito Aelio Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate dedicante Lucio Novio Crispino legato Augusti pro praetore consule designato possessores vici Verecundensis
EDCS-23100038.translation: To the divinity of the Augustan house and to the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, father of his country, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power; dedicated by Lucius Novius Crispinus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, designated consul, the inhabitants of the Vicus Verecundensis.
EDCS-23100038.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the residents of Vicus Verecundensis to the imperial house and to Emperor Antoninus Pius, overseen by the legate Lucius Novius Crispinus, dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 AD).

EDCS-23100042.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae per legionem III Augustam Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano legato Augusti pro praetore dedicante
EDCS-23100042.translation: The Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 23rd time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, through the Third Augustan Legion, with Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, dedicating it.
EDCS-23100042.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely for a public work or monument, carried out by the Third Augustan Legion and dedicated by his legate Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, dating to around 160 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-23100044.text_edition: Divo Antonino Augusto ex cuius indulgentia aqua vico Augustorum Verecundensium perducta est dedicante Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano legato Augusti pro praetore decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100044.translation: To the divine Antoninus Augustus, by whose indulgence water was brought to the village of the Augustan Verecundenses, with Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, dedicating it by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-23100044.summary: This inscription commemorates the construction of an aqueduct, financed by public funds and decreed by local decurions, which brought water to the village of Augustan Verecundenses through the generosity of Emperor Antoninus Augustus, and was dedicated by his legate Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus.

EDCS-23100067.text_edition: Marco Aemilio Dinarcho Marci Aemili Macri legati Augusti pro praetore clarissimi viri filio decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100067.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Dinarchus, son of Marcus Aemilius Macer, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, a most distinguished man, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-23100067.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aemilius Dinarchus, son of a distinguished imperial legate, made by decree of the local decurions using public funds, likely for a statue or public recognition.

EDCS-23100068.text_edition: Marco Aemilio Saturnino Marci Aemili Macri legati Augusti pro praetore clarissimi viri filio decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100068.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Saturninus, son of Marcus Aemilius Macer, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, a most distinguished man, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-23100068.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aemilius Saturninus, son of a distinguished imperial legate, made by decree of the local decurions using public funds, likely for a statue or public recognition.

EDCS-23100069.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Gordiano legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro patrono decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100069.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Gordianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, a most distinguished man, our patron, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-23100069.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Gordianus, a distinguished imperial legate and local patron, erected by decree of the decurions with public funds, likely honoring his patronage.

EDCS-23100070.text_edition: Numisiae Publi filiae Celerinae coniugi Decimi Fontei Frontiniani consularis patroni decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100070.translation: To Numisia Celerina, daughter of Publius, wife of Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, consular and patron, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-23100070.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Numisia Celerina, wife of a consular patron, erected by decree of the decurions with public funds, likely in recognition of her status or the family's contributions.

EDCS-23100157.text_edition: Pro salute Imppperatorum Caesssarum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini et Parthico sic maximi Augggustorum et Iuliae Augustae matri sic castrorum et Auggustorum totiusque domus divinae dedicante Subatiano Proculo legato Augggustorum sub cura Cai Iuli Paulini |(centurionis) cohortis II Maurorum vexillatio nummo collato numero CXXVI Kalendis Augustis domino nostro Antonino III et Geta II consulibus votum solvit libens animo
EDCS-23100157.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), Parthicus Maximus Augusti, and Julia Augusta, mother of the camps and Augusti, and of the entire divine household, with Subatianus Proculus, legate of the Augusti, dedicating it, under the care of Gaius Iulius Paulinus, centurion of the Second Cohort of Mauretanian auxiliaries; a vexillation (detachment) paid by contribution, numbering 126, on the Kalends of August, in the third consulship of our lord Antoninus and the second of Geta, willingly and with sincere intention fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-23100157.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by a vexillation of 126 soldiers from the Second Cohort of Mauretanian auxiliaries for the well-being of the imperial family, dedicated by the legate Subatianus Proculus and overseen by centurion Gaius Iulius Paulinus, dating to August 1, 211 CE, during the consulships of Caracalla and Geta.

EDCS-23100163.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Victor, a veteran ex-tesserarius (sergeant), born during the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos, lived for 80 years. While alive, he made this for himself and his wife.
EDCS-23100163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Victor, a veteran, who prepared this monument for himself and his wife during his lifetime, noting his birth date by consulship and his age at death.

EDCS-23100346.translation: In the most flourishing age of our lords the Emperors Caesar Maximinus Pius Felix and Maximus, most noble Caesar, Augusti, Gaius Iulius Saturninus Messapanus, son of Maximus, and Quintus Canneius Emeritus Aponianus, son of Quintus Canneius Gemellinus, who had promised a sundial to their fellow citizens of their own accord, gave and dedicated it at their own expense on the sixth day before the Kalends of April, during the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelius.
EDCS-23100346.summary: This inscription records the donation and dedication of a sundial by Gaius Iulius Saturninus Messapanus and Quintus Canneius Emeritus Aponianus to their fellow citizens, built at their own expense and dedicated on March 27, 237 CE, during the consulships of Perpetuus and Cornelius.

EDCS-23100409.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae sanctae Soli Mithrae Herculi Marti Mercurio Genio loci diis deabusque omnibus Marcus Aurelius Decimus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Numidiae ex principe peregrinorum votum solvit
EDCS-23100409.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva Sancta, Sol Mithras, Hercules, Mars, Mercury, the Genius of the place, and all gods and goddesses, Marcus Aurelius Decimus, a perfectissimus vir, governor of the province of Numidia, formerly princeps peregrinorum, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23100409.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Decimus, a former princeps peregrinorum and current governor of Numidia, fulfilling a vow to a wide pantheon of gods and goddesses, including Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, Sol Mithras, and the local Genius.

EDCS-23100413.text_edition: Victoriae Augustorum sacrum dedicante Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano legato Augustorum pro praetore ex testamento Marci Cossini Secundi flaminis perpetui ex HS IIII milibus nummum Lucius Sutorius Luci filius Papiria Felix flamen perpetuus heres eius posuit
EDCS-23100413.translation: Sacred to the Victory of the Augusti, dedicated by Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, from the will of Marcus Cossinius Secundus, perpetual flamen, from four thousand sesterces. Lucius Sutorius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, Felix, perpetual flamen, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-23100413.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Victory of the Emperors by Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, made on behalf of Marcus Cossinius Secundus by his heir, Lucius Sutorius Felix, using funds from Cossinius's will.

EDCS-23100414.translation: To the Parthian Victory of the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar (deleted). Titus Aurelius Fortis, aedile, duumvir, for the honor of his duumvirate, which he had promised with 4,000 sesterces, gave with amplified money in the year of his duumvirate, and also dedicated this. Dedicated on the Ides of May, during the consulship of Saturninus and Gallus.
EDCS-23100414.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Parthian Victory of the Imperial family (with Geta's name erased) by Titus Aurelius Fortis, commemorating his duumvirate and his donation of an amplified sum of money, erected in 202 CE.

EDCS-23100420.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto tribunicia potestate II consuli II divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano legato Augusti pro praetore consule designato patrono municipi decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23100420.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, son of Divus Antoninus, grandson of Divus Hadrianus, great-grandson of Divus Traianus Parthicus, great-great-grandson of Divus Nerva; to Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, consul designate, patron of the municipality, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23100420.summary: This inscription records a dedication, funded by public money and decreed by the decurions, to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus and to Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, a legate and patron of the municipality.

EDCS-23100430.text_edition: Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano Lucio Stertinio Rufino legato Augusti pro praetore consuli patrono municipi ordo Dianensium aere conlato
EDCS-23100430.translation: To Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, Lucius Stertinius Rufinus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, consul, patron of the municipality, the order of the Dianenses by collected money.
EDCS-23100430.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Dianenses, funded by collected money, to Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus Lucius Stertinius Rufinus, a legate, consul, and patron of their municipality.

EDCS-23100431.text_edition: Marco Valerio Maximiano Marci Valeri Maximiani quinquennalis sacerdotalis filio pontifici coloniae Poetovionensium legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro consuli designato nnissimo praesidi patrono Aquilius Restitutus flamen perpetuus aedilis IIvirum sic IIvir quinquennalis praefectus iure dicundo pro IIviris et Marcianus augur aedilis IIvirum sic sua pecunia fecerunt idemque dedicaverunt
EDCS-23100431.translation: To Marcus Valerius Maximianus, son of Marcus Valerius Maximianus, quinquennial sacerdotalis, pontifex of the colony of Poetovio, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, most distinguished man, consul designate, most renowned governor, patron. Aquilius Restitutus, perpetual flamen, aedile, duumvir, quinquennial duumvir, prefect for administering justice on behalf of the duumvirs, and Marcianus, augur, aedile, duumvir, made and dedicated this with their own money.
EDCS-23100431.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Valerius Maximianus, a prominent figure with numerous public offices, made and dedicated with their own money by Aquilius Restitutus and Marcianus.

EDCS-23200371.translation: Titus Arranius Peregrinus, son of Titus, of the Arniensis tribe, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, served for 12 years, lived for 30 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23200371.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Arranius Peregrinus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, who served 12 years and died at age 30.

EDCS-23200374.translation: Aelia Fortunata, daughter of Publius, lived for 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23200374.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Fortunata, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-23200386.translation: Iulia Fortunata lived piously for 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23200386.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, stating she lived piously for 40 years.

EDCS-23200390.translation: Marcia Maritima lived piously for 80 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23200390.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcia Maritima, who lived a pious life for 80 years.

EDCS-23200391.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Pompeius Euglycus, indicating he lived for three years.

EDCS-23200396.translation: Gaius Rubellius Niger lived for 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23200396.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Rubellius Niger, indicating his age and burial place.

EDCS-23200399.translation: Gaius Veturius Felix lived for 25 years.
EDCS-23200399.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Veturius Felix, stating his age at death.

EDCS-23600009.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinace sic Augusto Parthico Adiabenico Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Augusti nostri filio Lucio Septimio Getae Caesari Augusto Augusti nostri filio principi iuventutis procurante Rossio Vitulo egregio viro Pastor vilicus Cuiculi et Milei et Adauctus contrascriptor
EDCS-23600009.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Pius Augustus Parthicus Adiabenicus, to Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, to Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of our Augustus, to Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar Augustus, son of our Augustus, prince of youth; under the procuratorship of Rossi Vitulus, an eminent man. Pastor, bailiff of Cuicul and Milei, and Adauctus, co-clerk.
EDCS-23600009.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Severan imperial family (Septimius Severus, Julia Domna, Caracalla, and Geta) by two individuals, Pastor and Adauctus, under the procuratorship of Rossius Vitulus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-23600057.translation: Dedicated on the 13th day before the Kalends of August (July 20), when our lords Diocletian for the 4th time and Maximian for the 3rd time were Augusti and consuls.
EDCS-23600057.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument on July 20, 290 AD, during the consulship of Diocletian (for the 4th time) and Maximian (for the 3rd time).

EDCS-23600104.text_edition: Iuliae Titi filiae Chilonidi clarissimae feminae coniugi Tiberi Claudi Gordiani legati Augusti pro praetore clarissimi viri patroni coloniae decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23600104.translation: To Julia Chilonis, daughter of Titus, a most distinguished woman, wife of Tiberius Claudius Gordianus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, a most distinguished man, patron of the colony; by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23600104.summary: This inscription is an honorary dedication to Julia Chilonis, a distinguished woman and wife of a governor, erected by the colony of decurions using public funds.

EDCS-23600107.text_edition: Cnaeo Petronio Probato Iuniori Iusto legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro praesidi exempli rarissimi
EDCS-23600107.translation: To Gnaeus Petronius Probatus Junior Iustus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, a most distinguished man, governor of a most rare example.
EDCS-23600107.summary: This inscription is an honorary dedication to Gnaeus Petronius Probatus Junior Iustus, a governor praised for his exceptional qualities.

EDCS-23600117.translation: Dedicated on the 6th day before the Ides of July (July 10), when Dexter for the 2nd time and Priscus were consuls.
EDCS-23600117.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument on July 10, 215 AD, during the consulship of Dexter (for the second time) and Priscus.

EDCS-23600555.text_edition: Telluri Genetrici res publica Cuiculitanorum templum fecit Caius Iulius Lepidus Tertullus legatus Augusti pro praetore dedicavit simulacrum deae acrolithum Tiberius Iulius Honoratus pontifex flamen perpetuus dono dedit
EDCS-23600555.translation: To Mother Earth, the Republic of Cuiculitan created this temple. Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, dedicated it. Tiberius Julius Honoratus, perpetual pontiff and flamen, presented the acrolithic statue of the goddess as a gift.
EDCS-23600555.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to Mother Earth by the community of Cuicul, its dedication by the provincial governor Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, and the donation of the goddess's statue by Tiberius Julius Honoratus.

EDCS-23600591.translation: To the deified Antoninus, father of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus; by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23600591.summary: This inscription is a public dedication, by decree of the decurions, to the deified Antoninus Pius, father of Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, likely dating to the co-reign of his adoptive sons (c. 161-169 AD).

EDCS-23600606.text_edition: Genio populi Cuiculitanorum Lucius Claudius Tiberi filius Quirina Honoratus tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis praefectus cohortis I Augustae Pannoniorum equo publico exornatus ab Imperatore Antonino Augusto Pio in quinque decurias adlectus coloniae Cirtensis decurio et aedilis coloniae Cuiculitanae decurio et augur exedram cum statua et columnis marmoreis quam Claudius Modestus pater suus ob honorem pontificatus sine taxatione promiserat ex decreto Fontei Frontiniani legati Augusti pro praetore clarissimi viri ampliata pecunia sua fecit dedicavitque
EDCS-23600606.translation: To the Genius of the people of Cuicul: Lucius Claudius Honoratus, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, military tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, prefect of the First Augustan Cohort of Pannonians, honored with a public horse by Imperator Antoninus Augustus Pius, admitted to the five decuriae, decurion of the colony of Cirta, and decurion and augur of the colony of Cuiculitana, constructed and dedicated an exedra with a statue and marble columns, which his father Claudius Modestus had promised without taxation due to his pontificate, expanding the cost with his own money, by decree of Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-23600606.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of an exedra with a statue and marble columns to the Genius of the people of Cuicul by Lucius Claudius Honoratus, an individual with a distinguished military and civic career, fulfilling his father's earlier promise and adding his own funds, under the decree of governor Fonteius Frontinianus, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-23600667.translation: To Flavia Rufina, who lived for 11 years and 3 months. Valerius Narseus, her husband, and Romanus, her dearest son, made this for her.
EDCS-23600667.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a husband and son to Flavia Rufina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23700012.text_edition: Nundinae habentur hic in castello Mastarensi die III Kalendarum Septembrium primarum et die III Iduum Septembrium subsequentium et deinceps suo quoque mense ex permissu Marci Aureli Comini Cassiani legati Auggustorum pro praetore clarissimi viri
EDCS-23700012.translation: Fairs are held here in the fort of Mastarensis on the third day before the Kalends of September for the first fair, and on the third day before the Ides of September for the subsequent one, and thereafter in each month, by permission of Marcus Aurelius Cominius Cassianus, legate of the Augusti, propraetor, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-23700012.summary: This inscription records the official establishment of monthly fairs in the fort of Mastarensis, sanctioned by the imperial legate Marcus Aurelius Cominius Cassianus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-23700207.text_edition: Genio balineo castelli MASOM a solo quartum Idibus Decembribus Modesto et Probo consulibus
EDCS-23700207.translation: To the Genius of the bathhouse of the fort of Masom, from its foundation, on the fourth day before the Ides of December, in the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-23700207.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a bathhouse to the Genius of the fort of Masom, on December 10, 228 AD, when Modestus and Probus were consuls.

EDCS-23701756.translation: To the Divinity of Caelestis Augusta, for the Emperor Trajan Hadrian Caesar Augustus, with tribunician power, pontifex maximus, consul for the third time. Quintus Raecius Quadratus placed these temples and willingly assigned them to his heir or heirs for maintenance, on the 7th day before the Ides of September, in the consulship of Lucius Catillius Severus for the second time and Titus Aurelius Fulvius Boionius Antoninus, with Raecius Sodalis as curator.
EDCS-23701756.summary: This inscription records the dedication of temples to Caelestis Augusta by Quintus Raecius Quadratus for Emperor Hadrian, specifying their future care by his heirs, and is dated September 7, 131 AD.

EDCS-23701921.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods, the Great Idaean Holy Mother, made for the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and Julia Mamaea Augusta, and their divine house; because Claudius Basilicus, Amphio, and Mnesius performed a criobolium (a ram sacrifice) and undertook it themselves, through Gaius Aemilius Saturninus, priest, according to the prophecy of the archigallus. The place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23701921.summary: This inscription commemorates a criobolium (ram sacrifice) performed for the well-being of Emperor Severus Alexander, Julia Mamaea, and the imperial house by Claudius Basilicus, Amphio, and Mnesius, under the guidance of priest Gaius Aemilius Saturninus and an archigallus, with the site sanctioned by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-24100260.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Sittius Mustius, son of Lucius, who lived 70 years. [Illegible]
EDCS-24100260.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Sittius Mustius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-24100263.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Manius, veteran, lived 85 years.
EDCS-24100263.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Manius, a veteran, noting his age at death.

EDCS-24100274.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Arria Apollinia, who lived 105 years, she lies here.
EDCS-24100274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arria Apollinia, recording her exceptionally long life.

EDCS-24100276.translation: Quintus Caecilius ATRM, who lived 50 years, lies here.
EDCS-24100276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-24200382.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Rustica vixit annos
EDCS-24200382.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Julia Rustica lived for [illegible] years.
EDCS-24200382.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Rustica, noting her name and that she lived for an unspecified number of years.

EDCS-24300271.translation: To Terra Mater, to Herecura, to the Great Mother of the Gods, Idaea. Popilia Maxima, daughter of Marcus, placed, moved, and made this altar for a taurobolium.
EDCS-24300271.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar for a taurobolium ritual by Popilia Maxima to Terra Mater, Herecura, and the Great Mother of the Gods, Idaea.

EDCS-24400857.text_edition: Termini positi inter Igilgilitanos in quorum finibus kastellum Victoriae positum est et Zimizes ut sciant Zimizes non plus in usum se habere ex auctoritate Marci Vetti Latronis procuratoris Augusti qua in circuitu a muro kastelli passus D provinciae LXXXIX Torquato et Libone consulibus
EDCS-24400857.translation: Boundaries were established between the Igilgilitani, in whose territory the fort of Victoria is situated, and the Zimizes, so that the Zimizes may know that they are not to have more use of the land, by the authority of Marcus Vettius Latro, procurator of Augustus, than 500 paces in circuit from the fort wall, in the 89th province, in the consulship of Torquatus and Libo.
EDCS-24400857.summary: This inscription describes the establishment of land boundaries by the imperial procurator Marcus Vettius Latro between the Igilgilitani and the Zimizes in the 89th province, near the fort of Victoria, dated to 161 CE.

EDCS-24500177.text_edition: Nymphis Caius Prastina Messalinus legatus Augusti pro praetore consul designatus
EDCS-24500177.translation: To the Nymphs. Gaius Prastina Messalinus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, consul-designate.
EDCS-24500177.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by Gaius Prastina Messalinus, who held the titles of imperial legate and consul-designate.

EDCS-24500180.text_edition: Pro salute et victoria domini nostri Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pertenacis sic Augusti e[t] Clodi Albini Caesaris Aesculapem sic et Hygiam sic dedicante Lepido Tertullo legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro Marcus Oppius Antiochianus |(centurio) legionis III Augustae piae v[indicis] posuit et votum solvit
EDCS-24500180.translation: For the health and victory of our lord Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Clodius Albinus Caesar, to Aesculapius and Hygia, dedicated by Lepidus Tertullus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, most distinguished man. Marcus Oppius Antiochianus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, loyal and avenging, set this up and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24500180.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow and dedication by Marcus Oppius Antiochianus, a centurion, to Aesculapius and Hygia, for the well-being and victory of Emperors Septimius Severus and Clodius Albinus, under the authority of legate Lepidus Tertullus.

EDCS-24500268.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani nepote pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate VI consule III patre patriae plateam stratam Caius Prastina Messallinus legatus Augusti pro praetore legionis III Augustae patronus coloniae dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500268.translation: In the reign of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 6th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country: Gaius Prastina Messallinus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power of the Third Augustan Legion, patron of the colony, dedicated this paved street by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500268.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a paved street by Gaius Prastina Messallinus, imperial legate and patron of the colony, during the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius, funded by public money via a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-24500271.text_edition: Imperatore! Caesare sic divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepote sic divi Nervae pronepote sic Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifice sic maximo tribunicia potestate XV imperatore sic II consule! IIII patre sic patriae Marcus Valerius Etruscus legatus Augusti pro praetore patronus coloniae curiam renovatam et exornatam dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500271.translation: In the reign of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country: Marcus Valerius Etruscus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, patron of the colony, dedicated the renovated and adorned curia by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500271.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a renovated and adorned curia by Marcus Valerius Etruscus, imperial legate and patron of the colony, during the 15th tribunician year of Emperor Antoninus Pius (151-152 CE), financed by public funds.

EDCS-24500272.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XV imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae Marcus Valerius Etruscus legatus Augusti pro praetore patronus coloniae dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500272.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country: Marcus Valerius Etruscus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, patron of the colony, dedicated this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500272.summary: This inscription commemorates a public dedication by Marcus Valerius Etruscus, imperial legate and patron of the colony, to Emperor Antoninus Pius in his 15th tribunician year (151-152 CE), funded by public decree.

EDCS-24500273.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio Lucius Matuccius Fuscinus legatus consul designatus dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500273.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius: Lucius Matuccius Fuscinus, legate, consul-designate, dedicated this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500273.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius by Lucius Matuccius Fuscinus, imperial legate and consul-designate, funded by the decurions' decree.

EDCS-24500301.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Armeniaco Parthico Medico Germanico Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXVIII imperatori VII consuli III patri patriae proconsuli divi Antonini Pii filio divi Veri Parthici maximi fratri divi Hadriani nepos sic divi Traiani pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti aquamnetensem Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus legatus Augusti pro praetore consul patronus coloniae dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500301.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Armeniacus Parthicus Medicus Germanicus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 28th time, imperator for the 7th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, proconsul, son of divine Antoninus Pius, brother of divine Verus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of divine Hadrian, great-grandson of divine Trajan, great-great-grandson of divine Nerva: Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, consul, patron of the colony, dedicated the aqueduct by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500301.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an aqueduct by Marcus Aemilius Macer Saturninus, imperial legate and patron of the colony, to Emperor Marcus Aurelius in his 28th tribunician year (173-174 CE), funded by public decree.

EDCS-24500302.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Antonino Caesari imperatori destinato Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici vindicis et conditoris Romanae disciplinae filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti decreto decurionum pecunia publica Quintus Anicius Faustus legatus Augustorum pro praetore patronus coloniae dedicavit
EDCS-24500302.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, designated imperator, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, avenger and founder of Roman discipline, grandson of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus and divine Nerva: Quintus Anicius Faustus, legate of the Augusti with pro-praetorian power, patron of the colony, dedicated this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-24500302.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by Quintus Anicius Faustus, imperial legate and patron of the colony, to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar (Caracalla) during his designation as imperator, honoring his imperial lineage, funded by the decurions' decree.

EDCS-24500320.text_edition: Sexto Anicio Fausto Anici consularis filio Pauliniano clarissimo puero Quintus Tegonius Latinus Quinti Tegoni Saturnini flaminis perpetui filius patrono
EDCS-24500320.translation: To Sextus Anicius Faustus, son of the consular Anicius, Paulinianus, a most distinguished youth. Quintus Tegonius Latinus, son of Quintus Tegonius Saturninus, perpetual flamen, to his patron.
EDCS-24500320.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Tegonius Latinus to his patron, Sextus Anicius Faustus Paulinianus, honoring the distinguished young man.

EDCS-24500322.text_edition: Aulo Larcio Auli filio Quirina Prisco VIviro equitum Romanorum Xviro stlitibus iudicandis quaestori provinciae Asiae legato Augusti legionis leg III sic Scythicae pro=ED#pro#PED legato consulare sic provinciae Syriae tribuno plebis praetori praefecto frumenti dandi ex senatus consulto legato provinciae Baeticae Hispaniae proconsuli provinciae Galliae Narbonensis legato Augusti legionis II Augustae legato Augusti pro praetore exercitus provinciae Africae VIIviro epulonum consuli designato patrono coloniae decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-24500322.translation: To Aulus Larcius, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe, Priscus, sevir of Roman knights, decemvir for judging lawsuits, quaestor of the province of Asia, imperial legate of the Third Scythian Legion, propraetorian legate of the province of Syria, tribune of the plebs, praetor, prefect for the distribution of grain by decree of the Senate, legate of the province of Baetica Hispania, proconsul of the province of Gallia Narbonensis, imperial legate of the Second Augusta Legion, imperial legate pro praetore of the army of the province of Africa, septemvir of the epulones, consul designate, patron of the colony, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-24500322.summary: This is a public dedication by the decurions of a colony, funded by public money, honoring Aulus Larcius Priscus for his extensive public career and designating him as a patron of the colony.

EDCS-24700788.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XX imperatori III consuli III patri patriae  liberalissimoque? principi dedicante Publio Caelio Optato legato Augusti pro praetore |(centuriones) et veterani legionis III Augustae qui militare coeperunt divo Pio III et Marco Aurelio Vero II et Stloga et Severo consulibus
EDCS-24700788.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Armeniacus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the twentieth time, acclaimed Imperator for the third time, Consul for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, and most liberal prince, dedicated by Publius Caelius Optatus, imperial legate pro praetore. The centurions and veterans of the Third Augustan Legion who began their military service when the divine Pius was consul for the third time, and Marcus Aurelius Verus for the second time, and Stloga and Severus were consuls.
EDCS-24700788.summary: This is a dedication by the centurions and veterans of the Third Augustan Legion, under the direction of Publius Caelius Optatus, to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), likely celebrating his imperial titles and beneficence, with a retrospective mention of their enlistment date (around 161 AD).

EDCS-24700793.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Antonino Caesari imperatori destinato Quintus Anicius Faustus legatus Augustorum pro praetore consul designatus fecit
EDCS-24700793.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, designated emperor, Quintus Anicius Faustus, imperial legate pro praetore and consul designate, made this.
EDCS-24700793.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Quintus Anicius Faustus, an imperial legate and consul designate, to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, who was designated as emperor.

EDCS-24700801.text_edition: Publio Novio Saturnino Martiali Marcello Luci Novi Crispini legati Augusti pro praetore consulis designati filio speculatores
EDCS-24700801.translation: To Publius Novius Saturninus Martialis Marcellus, son of Lucius Novius Crispinus, imperial legate pro praetore and consul designate, from the speculatores.
EDCS-24700801.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the speculatores (scouts/bodyguards) to Publius Novius Saturninus Martialis Marcellus, son of a high-ranking official.

EDCS-24700812.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti tribunicia potestate XVI consuli III primi ordines et centuriones et evocatus legionis III Augustae dedicante Decimo Fonteio Frontiniano legato Augusti pro praetore consule designato
EDCS-24700812.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, Consul for the third time, by the first ranks and centurions and evocatus of the Third Augustan Legion, dedicated by Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, imperial legate pro praetore and consul designate.
EDCS-24700812.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the officers and veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, under the direction of Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), highlighting his imperial lineage and titles (dating to around 213 AD).

EDCS-24800676.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum dedicante Lucio Novio Crispino legato Augusti pro praetore Caius Antonius Cai filius Collina Alexander Antiochia curiae Hadrianae felici veteranorum legionis III Augustae ob honorem flaminatus perpetui quod in se absentem contulerunt promissa statua ex HS IIII milibus nummum ampliata pecunia fecit
EDCS-24800676.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Dedicated by Lucius Novius Crispinus, imperial legate pro praetore. Caius Antonius, son of Caius, of the Collina tribe, Alexander, from Antioch, of the fortunate Hadriana curia of the veterans of the Third Augustan Legion, on account of the honor of perpetual flaminate which they conferred upon him in his absence, made this promised statue, augmented from 4,000 sesterces.
